city-centre.tip: Visit on weekday evenings for easier bar hopping and better deals. Most venues offer happy hour specials between 5-8pm before the weekend crowds arrive.

city-centre.name: Donghu Park （North Gate）
city-centre.address: 299 Er Huan Lu Dong Wu Duan, Jin Jiang Qu, Cheng Du Shi, Si Chuan Sheng, China, 610021
city-centre.description: This urban park features a serene lake surrounded by willow trees and traditional pavilions, popular with local Chengdu residents practicing tai chi and playing mahjong. The peaceful morning atmosphere offers an authentic glimpse into everyday Chengdu life away from the tourist crowds.
city-centre.tip: Visit early morning around 7-8am to see elderly locals doing synchronized exercises and hear traditional Chinese instruments being played near the lakeside pavilions.

city-centre.name: Baihuatan Park
city-centre.address: 175 Yi Huan Lu Xi Yi Duan, Qing Yang Qu, Cheng Du Shi, Si Chuan Sheng, China, 610032
city-centre.description: This historic Chengdu park combines classical Sichuan gardens with peaceful riverside paths along the Funan River, featuring ancient pavilions and bamboo groves. The traditional teahouse overlooking the lotus pond offers an authentic glimpse into local leisure culture where residents gather daily for tea and mahjong.
city-centre.tip: Visit the west side early morning to watch locals practice tai chi by the water, then grab a cup of covered bowl tea at the waterside teahouse for around 15-20 RMB.

jinniu-north.name: Huanglongxi Ancient Town Tourism Area （Northeast Gate）
jinniu-north.chinese: 黄龙溪古镇旅游区（东北门）
jinniu-north.address: China, Si Chuan Sheng, Cheng Du Shi, Shuang Liu Qu, 双黄路 邮政编码: 610220
jinniu-north.summary: Cobblestone streets with Ming- & Qing-era stilted buildings, plus ancient temples & a water mill.
jinniu-north.description: Wander cobblestone lanes lined with authentic Ming and Qing dynasty stilted buildings, where a centuries-old water mill still turns beside ancient temples. The remarkably preserved architecture transports you to imperial China without the overwhelming crowds of more famous ancient towns.
jinniu-north.tip: Visit the traditional teahouses along the riverbank early morning when locals gather for performances of Sichuan opera and shadow puppetry, often free to watch.

jinniu-north.name: Chengdu Panda Museum
jinniu-north.address: China, Si Chuan Sheng, Cheng Du Shi, Cheng Hua Qu, Xiong Mao Da Dao, 1375号成都大熊猫繁育研究基地 邮政编码: 610083
jinniu-north.description: This specialized museum traces the evolutionary history of giant pandas through fossils, multimedia displays, and interactive exhibits explaining conservation efforts. Visit to understand the scientific research behind panda breeding programs and see how these endangered creatures are being saved from extinction.
jinniu-north.tip: Visit the museum before or after seeing live pandas at the adjacent Chengdu Research Base of Giant Panda Breeding to maximize your understanding of these animals.

jinniu-north.name: Wetland Park
jinniu-north.address: QW3H+88H, Dan Gui Lu, Pi Du Qu, Cheng Du Shi, Si Chuan Sheng, China, 611731
jinniu-north.description: This wetland sanctuary in Jinniu's northern reaches features boardwalk trails winding through reed beds where migratory birds rest during spring and autumn migrations. The peaceful waterways and native vegetation offer a quick escape from urban Chengdu without leaving the city limits.
jinniu-north.tip: Visit early morning between 6-8am when bird activity peaks and local photographers gather at the eastern observation deck for the best wildlife spotting opportunities.

jinniu-north.name: Chengdu City Plant Park
jinniu-north.address: China, Si Chuan Sheng, Cheng Du Shi, Jin Niu Qu, 川陕路Q47F+RFW 邮政编码: 610503
jinniu-north.description: This expansive botanical garden showcases diverse plant collections including bamboo groves, rose gardens, and traditional Chinese medicinal herbs across themed sections. The peaceful lake surrounded by willow trees and lotus ponds offers a serene escape from Chengdu's urban bustle.
jinniu-north.tip: Visit the bonsai exhibition hall near the west gate to see miniature specimens over 100 years old, often overlooked by tourists who stick to the main paths.

jinniu-north.name: Chengdu Research Base of Giant Panda Breeding Giant Panda Brood Area
jinniu-north.chinese: 成都大熊猫繁育研究基地大熊猫产房区
jinniu-north.address: P4QR+M2Q, Xindu District, Chengdu, Sichuan, China, 610083
jinniu-north.description: Watch newborn pandas, some just days old, sleeping in incubators and being bottle-fed by researchers in this intimate nursery area. The chance to see baby pandas at their most vulnerable and adorable stage makes this the most coveted section of the entire base.
jinniu-north.tip: Arrive right at 8:30am opening time and head straight to the nursery first—baby pandas sleep most of the day and crowds become overwhelming by 10am.

jinniu-north.name: 成都公园
jinniu-north.address: P454+MMW, Chenghua District, Chengdu, Sichuan, China, 610081
jinniu-north.description: This neighborhood park in Jinniu district offers a slice of everyday Chengdu life, where locals gather for tai chi, dancing, and teahouse sessions under mature trees. The relaxed atmosphere and authentic community vibe provide an unhurried glimpse into how residents spend their leisure time.
jinniu-north.tip: Visit early morning around 7-8am to watch elderly residents practice tai chi, ballroom dancing, and traditional exercises—bring comfortable shoes if you want to join in.

jinniu-north.name: Huanglongxi Scenic Spot
jinniu-north.description: This ancient river town features well-preserved Qing dynasty architecture along cobblestone streets, where traditional teahouses overlook the meeting point of two rivers. The lively waterfront area lets you watch locals playing mahjong while kids splash in the shallow river shallows.
jinniu-north.tip: Visit the noodle shops on the back streets away from the main square for authentic Sichuan dan dan mian at half the tourist-area price.

jinniu-north.name: Sichuan Cuisine Museum of Chengdu
jinniu-north.address: China, Cheng Du Shi, Pi Du Qu, 四川省成都市郫县古城镇荣华北巷8号 邮政编码: 611741
jinniu-north.summary: Spacious museum of Sichuan cuisine & culture, featuring exhibits, cooking classes & more.
jinniu-north.description: This interactive museum brings Sichuan's fiery culinary heritage to life through traditional kitchen exhibits, ancient cooking tools, and demonstrations of classic techniques like knife skills and wok-tossing. The hands-on cooking classes let you master iconic dishes like mapo tofu and kung pao chicken under the guidance of experienced chefs.
jinniu-north.tip: Book the morning cooking class session which includes lunch—you'll eat what you cook and the ingredients are freshest early in the day.

jinniu-north.name: Chengdu Research Base of Giant Panda Breeding No.14 Giant Panda Enclosure
jinniu-north.chinese: 成都大熊猫繁育研究基地14号大熊猫圈舍
jinniu-north.address: China, Si Chuan Sheng, Cheng Du Shi, Cheng Hua Qu, Xiong Mao Da Dao, 1375号成都大熊猫繁育研究基地 邮政编码: 610083
jinniu-north.description: This dedicated enclosure houses giant pandas in naturalistic bamboo-filled habitats where you can observe their feeding rituals and playful interactions up close. The intimate viewing platforms offer unparalleled opportunities to watch these gentle creatures lounging, munching, or tumbling about in their carefully designed environment.
jinniu-north.tip: Visit between 8-10am when pandas are most active during feeding time. Position yourself at the lower viewing deck for eye-level photography without glass reflections.

pidu-west.name: Jinsha Site Museum Tourist Center
pidu-west.chinese: 金沙遗址博物馆游客中心
pidu-west.address: 2 Jin Sha Yi Zhi Lu, Qing Yang Qu, Cheng Du Shi, Si Chuan Sheng, China, 610074
pidu-west.summary: Museum displaying the ancient finds of the Jinsha Site, including ivory, gold & jade artifacts.
pidu-west.description: This museum showcases spectacular archaeological treasures from the 3,000-year-old Jinsha civilization, including the famous Sun Bird gold foil emblem that became China's cultural heritage symbol. The climate-controlled exhibition halls preserve delicate ivory tusks, intricate jade artifacts, and ceremonial bronze objects that reveal the sophistication of ancient Shu culture.
pidu-west.tip: Visit the Relics Hall first when it opens to avoid tour groups, then head to the outdoor ecological garden where original excavation sites remain visible beneath protective glass pavilions.

pidu-west.name: Shiren Park
pidu-west.address: 2 Shi Ren Bei Lu, Qing Yang Qu, Cheng Du Shi, Si Chuan Sheng, China, 610074
pidu-west.description: This local neighborhood park in Pidu District features open lawns, tree-lined paths, and exercise areas where Chengdu residents practice tai chi and badminton. The relaxed atmosphere offers an authentic glimpse into everyday life away from the tourist crowds.
pidu-west.tip: Visit early morning to see locals doing group exercises and traditional Chinese dancing, or bring a kite on weekends when families gather on the central lawn.

pidu-west.name: Jinsha Site Museum （North Gate）
pidu-west.address: 3 Jin Bo Lu, 金沙商圈 Jin Niu Qu, Cheng Du Shi, Si Chuan Sheng, China, 610036
pidu-west.description: This archaeological museum preserves a 3,000-year-old Shu Kingdom capital site with intact excavation pits, ancient ivory, and the famous golden Sun Bird emblem. The open-air ruins section reveals ancient residential foundations and sacrifice areas exactly where archaeologists discovered them.
pidu-west.tip: Enter through the North Gate to avoid crowds and start at the Relic Hall first, where you can watch ongoing archaeological restoration work through glass windows before exploring the outdoor excavation sites.

pidu-west.name: Huanhuaxi Park （North Gate 1）
pidu-west.address: 9 Qing Hua Lu, 青羊宫商圈 Qing Yang Qu, Cheng Du Shi, Si Chuan Sheng, China, 610032
pidu-west.description: This expansive urban park along Huanhua Creek features traditional pavilions, bamboo groves, and winding waterways that once inspired Tang Dynasty poet Du Fu. The lush gardens offer a peaceful escape from city noise, with locals practicing tai chi beside lotus ponds and stone bridges.
pidu-west.tip: Enter through the north gate early morning to join free tai chi sessions with regulars who gather near the main pond—they're welcoming to curious visitors.

pidu-west.name: 杜甫草堂
pidu-west.address: 28 Cao Tang Lu, Qing Yang Qu, Cheng Du Shi, Si Chuan Sheng, China, 610001
pidu-west.summary: The 20-hectare park & museum honoring Du Fu contains a replica of his cottage & copies of his works.
pidu-west.description: This tranquil 20-hectare park preserves the thatched cottage where Tang Dynasty poet Du Fu wrote over 240 poems during his four-year refuge in Chengdu. The meticulously maintained gardens with bamboo groves and koi ponds offer a serene escape that captures the contemplative spirit of classical Chinese poetry.
pidu-west.tip: Visit the Gongbu Shrine first thing in the morning when calligraphy enthusiasts gather to practice by the pond, offering authentic cultural moments before tour groups arrive.

pidu-west.name: Du Fu Thatched Cottage （Southwest Gate）
pidu-west.address: M25G+QJV, Cao Tang Lu, 青羊宫商圈 Qing Yang Qu, Cheng Du Shi, Si Chuan Sheng, China, 610074
pidu-west.description: This serene garden complex preserves the traditional thatched cottage where Tang Dynasty poet Du Fu lived during his exile, featuring classical pavilions, bamboo groves, and quiet ponds. The tranquil grounds offer a rare glimpse into ancient Chinese literary culture and traditional Sichuan garden design.
pidu-west.tip: Enter through the Southwest Gate to avoid tour groups who typically use the main entrance. Visit the Gongbu Shrine first thing in the morning for the best natural lighting for photos.

dujiangyan-day-trip.name: Dujiangyan Scenic Area （South Gate）
dujiangyan-day-trip.address: XJW6+P8J, Dujiangyan City, Chengdu, Sichuan, China, 611845
dujiangyan-day-trip.description: This 2,300-year-old irrigation system still waters Chengdu's plains, featuring ancient engineering marvels like the Fish Mouth Levee that divides the Minjiang River. The South Gate entrance offers direct access to Anlan Suspension Bridge and the best viewpoints of the functioning waterworks that transformed Sichuan into China's breadbasket.
dujiangyan-day-trip.tip: Visit the Erwang Temple first thing after entering through South Gate for crowd-free photos, then walk downhill following the water flow to understand the system's genius design.

dujiangyan-day-trip.name: Dujiangyanshui Culture Square
dujiangyan-day-trip.address: China, Si Chuan Sheng, Cheng Du Shi, Du Jiang Yan Shi, 江东街XJVF+838 邮政编码: 611830
dujiangyan-day-trip.description: This expansive public square celebrates the 2,000-year legacy of the Dujiangyan irrigation system through water-themed sculptures, interactive fountains, and cultural installations. It serves as the perfect orientation point before exploring the ancient waterworks, offering panoramic views of the surrounding mountains and river.
dujiangyan-day-trip.tip: Visit in early morning when locals practice tai chi and traditional dance by the water features—it's the best time for authentic cultural immersion and photos without crowds.

dujiangyan-day-trip.name: Dujiangyan Lingyanshan Scenic Area
dujiangyan-day-trip.address: 2J47+46X, Bai Hua Ling Lu, Du Jiang Yan Shi, Cheng Du Shi, Si Chuan Sheng, China, 611845
dujiangyan-day-trip.description: This forested mountain sanctuary offers hiking trails through bamboo groves and ancient temples carved into limestone cliffs, with sweeping views of the Dujiangyan irrigation system below. The chance to explore peaceful temple complexes away from tourist crowds while getting a bird's-eye perspective of the UNESCO-listed engineering marvel makes the climb worthwhile.
dujiangyan-day-trip.tip: Start your hike early morning to avoid afternoon heat and catch monks during their morning rituals at the clifftop temples around 7-8am.

dujiangyan-day-trip.name: Dujiangyan Scenic Area （Southeast Gate 2）
dujiangyan-day-trip.address: China, Si Chuan Sheng, Cheng Du Shi, Du Jiang Yan Shi, Gong Yuan Lu, 公园路
dujiangyan-day-trip.description: This southeastern entrance provides direct access to the ancient Dujiangyan irrigation system, a 2,000-year-old engineering marvel that still waters Chengdu's plains today. Walk along the canals to witness how ingenious bamboo and stone structures control the Minjiang River's powerful flow without modern technology.
dujiangyan-day-trip.tip: Enter through Southeast Gate 2 to avoid main entrance crowds and get quicker access to the Fish Mouth Levee, the system's most impressive engineering feature.

dujiangyan-day-trip.name: Chengdu Panda Breeding Research Center Dujiangyan Breeding Yefang Research Center
dujiangyan-day-trip.chinese: 成都大熊猫繁育研究基地都江堰繁育野放研究中心
dujiangyan-day-trip.address: XH6R+W6V, Dujiangyan City, Chengdu, Sichuan, China, 611843
dujiangyan-day-trip.summary: Wildlife facility dedicated to panda preservation, with animal viewing & feeding sessions.
dujiangyan-day-trip.description: This specialized facility focuses on rewilding giant pandas, offering a more naturalistic setting than typical breeding centers where bears roam semi-wild habitats across forested hillsides. Watch young pandas learning survival skills in spacious enclosures designed to prepare them for eventual release into protected mountain reserves.
dujiangyan-day-trip.tip: Visit during morning feeding times around 9-10am when pandas are most active and keepers demonstrate bamboo preparation techniques that differ from standard breeding center routines.

dujiangyan-day-trip.name: Guanxian County Ancient City Resort
dujiangyan-day-trip.address: China, Si Chuan Sheng, Cheng Du Shi, Du Jiang Yan Shi, 207, 正北方向50米 邮政编码: 611830
dujiangyan-day-trip.description: This riverside heritage district preserves the architectural charm of old Guanxian (now Dujiangyan) with traditional Qing Dynasty buildings, ancient stone streets, and views of the Min River. The peaceful riverside walkways and historic teahouses offer a glimpse into local Sichuanese life away from the main tourist circuit.
dujiangyan-day-trip.tip: Visit the small local temple near the east gate early morning to see residents practicing tai chi and offering incense—it's rarely crowded and gives authentic cultural insight.

dujiangyan-day-trip.name: Chengdu Rongchuang Culture Tour Cheng
dujiangyan-day-trip.address: China, Si Chuan Sheng, Cheng Du Shi, Du Jiang Yan Shi, 200, 米东北方向180米 邮政编码: 611843
dujiangyan-day-trip.description: This massive entertainment complex combines a snow and water park, shopping mall, and cultural performances under one roof in Chengdu's modern district. The indoor snow park offers year-round skiing and snowboarding in subtropical Sichuan, making it a unique escape from the city's summer heat.
dujiangyan-day-trip.tip: Visit on weekdays to avoid massive crowds, especially at the snow park. Book combo tickets online in advance for significant discounts on multiple attractions within the complex.

dujiangyan-day-trip.name: Qinyan Tower
dujiangyan-day-trip.description: This reconstructed historic tower stands as a tribute to the ancient engineering legacy of Dujiangyan's irrigation system, offering elevated views over the surrounding waterworks and mountains. The architectural details showcase traditional Qing dynasty design, making it a perfect photo stop during your Dujiangyan exploration.
dujiangyan-day-trip.tip: Visit in late afternoon when the light hits the tower's red columns beautifully and fewer tour groups crowd the viewing platform.

dujiangyan-day-trip.name: 南桥
dujiangyan-day-trip.address: XJW8+PC9, Nan Jie, Du Jiang Yan Shi, Cheng Du Shi, Si Chuan Sheng, China, 611830
dujiangyan-day-trip.description: This reconstructed Qing Dynasty covered bridge spans the Min River at Dujiangyan with elaborate wooden architecture, painted beams, and traditional teahouses built into its structure. The bridge offers an excellent vantage point to view the ancient irrigation system and surrounding mountains while experiencing traditional Sichuan bridge architecture.
dujiangyan-day-trip.tip: Visit in early morning when local tai chi practitioners gather on the bridge, and stop at the small teahouse on the west end for freshly made gaiwan tea with river views.

dujiangyan-day-trip.name: Daojie Dujiangyan
dujiangyan-day-trip.description: This national park section of Dujiangyan offers serene riverside trails where ancient waterworks meet lush mountain scenery, providing a quieter alternative to the main irrigation system viewing areas. The peaceful walking paths alongside rushing waters offer an intimate look at how nature and 2,000-year-old engineering coexist in harmony.
dujiangyan-day-trip.tip: Visit early morning when local tai chi practitioners gather near the water channels—you'll experience the park's contemplative atmosphere before tour groups arrive at the main Dujiangyan sites.

dujiangyan-day-trip.name: Dujiangyan Scenic Area （Northeast Gate）
dujiangyan-day-trip.description: This UNESCO-listed ancient irrigation system, built in 256 BC, still waters Chengdu's plains using ingenious dam-free engineering that channels the Min River through bamboo-lined pathways. Walking across the Anlan Suspension Bridge offers thrilling views of the rushing waters while experiencing a 2,000-year-old marvel that transformed Sichuan into the land of abundance.
dujiangyan-day-trip.tip: Enter through the Northeast Gate to avoid tour groups who cluster at the main entrance, giving you quieter morning access to the Fish Mouth Levee and better photo opportunities.

dujiangyan-day-trip.name: Tianfuyuan
dujiangyan-day-trip.address: China, Cheng Du Shi, Du Jiang Yan Shi, CN 四川省 成都市 都江堰市 商业街 33 33号南侧 邮政编码: 611830
dujiangyan-day-trip.description: This tranquil garden estate near Dujiangyan showcases traditional Sichuan architecture with elegant courtyards, koi ponds, and carefully manicured landscaping that reflects classical Chinese garden design. The peaceful atmosphere and authentic architectural details offer a serene escape from the busier tourist sites in the area.
dujiangyan-day-trip.tip: Visit during weekday mornings when the gardens are quietest and the soft light creates perfect reflections in the koi ponds for photography.

sanxingdui.name: Sanxingdui
sanxingdui.summary: Excavation site yielding bronze heads & sculptures dating from the 12th & 11th centuries BCE.
sanxingdui.description: Mysterious bronze masks with bulging eyes and enormous sculptures unearthed from this 3,000-year-old Shu Kingdom site challenge everything historians thought they knew about ancient Chinese civilization. The museum's newly excavated treasures, including a gold mask and jade artifacts, reveal a sophisticated culture that developed independently from the Yellow River civilizations.
sanxingdui.tip: Visit Hall 3 first when crowds are lighter to see the most impressive bronzes up close, including the 3.96-meter tall Bronze Sacred Tree without jostling for photos.

sanxingdui.name: 三星堆博物馆
sanxingdui.address: China, Si Chuan Sheng, De Yang Shi, Guang Han Shi, 丰富南兴镇真武村 邮政编码: 618323
sanxingdui.summary: Archaeology museum displaying remarkable 11th–12th century BCE finds from local excavations.
sanxingdui.description: This archaeological museum showcases mysterious Bronze Age artifacts including towering bronze masks, giant golden scepters, and otherworldly figurines unearthed from the ancient Shu civilization. The alien-like bronze heads with protruding eyes and elaborate gold masks offer a glimpse into a culture that challenges everything known about early Chinese history.
sanxingdui.tip: Visit the newer No. 2 exhibition hall first to see the most impressive artifacts including the famous bronze sacred tree, then work backwards chronologically for better context.

sanxingdui.name: Sanxing Dui Bowuguan
sanxingdui.description: This archaeological museum displays mysterious bronze masks, towering statues, and golden artifacts from a 3,000-year-old civilization that defied traditional Chinese history. The otherworldly relics—unlike anything found elsewhere in China—reveal an advanced ancient Shu culture that vanished without explanation.
sanxingdui.tip: Visit the newly opened second hall first when crowds are lighter, then work backwards. The most famous giant bronze masks are displayed here with better lighting than the older galleries.

sanxingdui.name: Sanxingdui Site
sanxingdui.description: This archaeological site unveiled mysterious bronze masks and towering golden staffs from a 3,000-year-old civilization that challenged everything historians knew about ancient China. The newly opened museum halls showcase otherworldly artifacts that look more alien than human, including the famous protruding-eyed bronze figures.
sanxingdui.tip: Visit the new museum halls first thing in the morning before tour groups arrive. The No. 3 sacrificial pit section with recent 2020-2021 discoveries is less crowded on weekdays.

sanxingdui.name: Sanxingdui Nation Archaeology Relics Park
sanxingdui.chinese: 三星堆国家考古遗址公园
sanxingdui.description: Discover bronze masks with protruding eyes, towering golden staffs, and jade artifacts from the mysterious Shu civilization that flourished 3,000 years ago. These enigmatic relics challenge everything historians thought they knew about ancient Chinese culture and artistic capabilities.
sanxingdui.tip: Visit the sacrificial pit exhibition halls first thing in the morning before tour groups arrive—the dramatic lighting on the bronze heads creates the best photo opportunities and viewing experience.

sanxingdui.name: Tianfu Square
sanxingdui.description: This vast central plaza features a massive statue of Chairman Mao and sits directly above a major metro interchange, surrounded by museums and the Sichuan Science and Technology Museum. The underground shopping mall beneath offers a climate-controlled escape and connects seamlessly to multiple attractions.
sanxingdui.tip: Visit the free Sichuan Science and Technology Museum on the north side of the square, which most tourists overlook but offers excellent air conditioning and fascinating exhibits.

sanxingdui.name: 四川科技馆
sanxingdui.address: 16 Ren Min Zhong Lu Yi Duan, Luomashi, Qing Yang Qu, Cheng Du Shi, Si Chuan Sheng, China, 610020
sanxingdui.summary: Large state museum featuring diverse science & technology exhibits, including airplanes & robots.
sanxingdui.description: This expansive science museum showcases interactive exhibits ranging from suspended aircraft and robotic demonstrations to hands-on physics experiments that engage visitors of all ages. The robotics hall, where programmable machines perform synchronized routines and visitors can test their coding skills, offers the most memorable experience.
sanxingdui.tip: Visit on weekday mornings to avoid school groups that fill the interactive zones after 2pm. The aerospace section on the third floor is least crowded and has English explanations.

standalone.name: Leshan Giant Buddha
standalone.address: 2345 Ling Yun Lu, Shi Zhong Qu, Le Shan Shi, Si Chuan Sheng, China, 614099
standalone.summary: Immense statue (built 713 to 803) that's carved out of a stone cliff & depicts a sitting Buddha.
standalone.description: This 71-meter-tall Tang Dynasty Buddha carved into a riverside cliff took 90 years to complete and remains the world's largest pre-modern stone sculpture. Watch how the ingenious drainage system, hidden in the hair and clothing, has protected this 1,300-year-old marvel from erosion.
standalone.tip: Skip the crowded boat tours and climb down the steep pathway along the cliff face to appreciate the statue's true scale and intricate details up close.

forbidden-city-core.name: The Palace Museum
forbidden-city-core.address: 4 Jing Shan Qian Jie, Dong Cheng Qu, Bei Jing Shi, China, 100009
forbidden-city-core.summary: Massive palace complex & museum featuring tours, art exhibits & restaurants.
forbidden-city-core.description: Home to nearly a million imperial treasures spanning 5,000 years of Chinese civilization, this former residence of 24 emperors houses jade carvings, ancient scrolls, and ceremonial robes within 980 surviving buildings. Walking through the central axis from the Meridian Gate to the Imperial Garden reveals the precise architectural hierarchy that governed imperial life for over 500 years.
forbidden-city-core.tip: Enter through the less crowded East Gate (Donghuamen) and visit the Treasure Gallery and Clock Gallery first before crowds arrive from the main southern entrance.

forbidden-city-core.name: Jingshan Park
forbidden-city-core.address: 44 Jing Shan Xi Jie, 景山 Xi Cheng Qu, Bei Jing Shi, China, 100009
forbidden-city-core.summary: Historic 57-acre park located on a hill & offering views of the Forbidden City.
forbidden-city-core.description: This 57-acre imperial garden crowns a man-made hill constructed from earth excavated during the Forbidden City's creation, with five pavilions dotting its summit ridge. Climb to the top for the most spectacular aerial perspective of the entire Forbidden City complex spread below, revealing its symmetrical architecture and golden rooftops.
forbidden-city-core.tip: Visit at sunset when tour groups have left and golden hour light bathes the Forbidden City. Enter from the north gate to avoid crowds and start your climb immediately.

forbidden-city-core.name: Tiananmen Square
forbidden-city-core.summary: Historic, sprawling city square & cultural center, featuring a 37m-tall obelisk.
forbidden-city-core.description: The world's largest public square stretches across 440,000 square meters, flanked by monuments that witnessed pivotal moments in modern Chinese history. Watch the daily flag-raising ceremony at dawn as soldiers march in perfect synchronization to raise the national flag before crowds of thousands.
forbidden-city-core.tip: Arrive 90 minutes before sunrise for flag-raising ceremony and pass through security on the square's north side, which moves fastest. Bring your passport for mandatory checks.

forbidden-city-core.name: Nine Dragon Screen
forbidden-city-core.address: W9JQ+V3C, Jiao Chang Hu Tong, 西安门 Xi Cheng Qu, Bei Jing Shi, China, 100035
forbidden-city-core.description: This 29.4-meter glazed tile screen wall features nine writhing dragons in bold yellows, blues, and greens, making it the largest of its kind in the Forbidden City. The intricate relief work and vivid colors have survived centuries, offering photographers exceptional detail in natural light.
forbidden-city-core.tip: Visit in late afternoon when tour groups thin out and angled sunlight brings out the depth of the carved dragons, making colors more vibrant for photos.

forbidden-city-core.name: Jiaolou
forbidden-city-core.description: These elegant watchtowers anchor each corner of the Forbidden City's walls, featuring intricate multi-eaved architecture with 28 ridges and 72 roof edges that appear to float like pagodas. The northwestern tower offers the most photogenic angle, especially reflected in the palace moat at sunrise or sunset.
forbidden-city-core.tip: Visit the northwestern corner tower from outside the palace walls near Shenwu Gate for the classic moat reflection photo without needing a Forbidden City ticket.

forbidden-city-core.name: Gate of Divine Prowess
forbidden-city-core.summary: Renovated in the 17th century, the gate offers a typical architecture with columns & 2 roofs.
forbidden-city-core.description: The north gate of the Forbidden City showcases elegant Qing Dynasty architecture with double-eaved roofs and crimson pillars, serving as the emperor's private entrance and exit. This is your best vantage point for viewing the palace complex from behind, offering a quieter alternative to the crowded southern gates.
forbidden-city-core.tip: Exit through this gate to reach Jingshan Park's south entrance directly across the street, where you can climb the hill for panoramic views of the entire Forbidden City.

forbidden-city-core.name: 太和门
forbidden-city-core.address: Gu Gong Bo Wu Yuan, Dong Cheng Qu, China, 100006
forbidden-city-core.description: This imposing gateway serves as the formal entrance to the outer court of the Forbidden City, where emperors once received officials during morning audiences. The massive wooden doors and intricate roof details showcase Ming Dynasty architectural mastery that has survived for six centuries.
forbidden-city-core.tip: Arrive right at opening time to photograph the gate without crowds blocking the view, and notice the original bronze lion statues flanking the entrance—touch their paws for good luck.

forbidden-city-core.name: Zhenbaoguan
forbidden-city-core.description: This treasure gallery within the Forbidden City showcases imperial jewelry, jade carvings, gold vessels, and ceremonial objects from China's last dynasty. The intricate craftsmanship of the empress dowager's personal ornaments and rare jade collection offers an intimate glimpse into royal opulence.
forbidden-city-core.tip: Visit right at opening time to avoid tour groups that typically arrive mid-morning, allowing you to photograph the smaller exhibit halls without crowds.

forbidden-city-core.name: Broken Rainbow Bridge
forbidden-city-core.address: China, Bei Jing Shi, Dong Cheng Qu, 米 邮政编码: 100034
forbidden-city-core.description: This ancient stone bridge with its distinctive weathered arch spans a quiet canal within the Forbidden City complex, offering a glimpse of Ming dynasty engineering. The bridge's eroded surface and moss-covered stones create an atmospheric contrast to the polished grandeur of the surrounding palace buildings.
forbidden-city-core.tip: Visit early morning when tour groups concentrate on main halls—the bridge area remains peaceful and the low light beautifully highlights the stone's aged texture.

forbidden-city-core.name: Meridian Gate
forbidden-city-core.address: 4 Jing Shan Qian Jie, Dong Cheng Qu, China, 100006
forbidden-city-core.summary: Imposing gateway & former ceremonial entrance to the Forbidden City for Ming & Qing Emperors.
forbidden-city-core.description: This massive five-pavilion gateway served as the ceremonial entrance where emperors reviewed their armies and announced the lunar calendar. Stand beneath its 38-meter height to appreciate the architectural power that once separated commoners from the emperor's realm.
forbidden-city-core.tip: Visit early morning right at opening to photograph the gate without crowds, and look for the hidden side passages where officials once entered by rank.

forbidden-city-core.name: Imperial Ancestral Temple
forbidden-city-core.summary: Temple cluster on 3 courtyards, site of historic sacrificial ceremonies & worship halls for royalty.
forbidden-city-core.description: This Ming dynasty temple complex features three magnificent courtyards where emperors performed sacred ancestral rites, surrounded by ancient cypress trees that have witnessed six centuries of imperial history. The serene atmosphere and remarkably preserved ceremonial halls offer an intimate glimpse into royal religious life often missed by tourists rushing to the adjacent Forbidden City.
forbidden-city-core.tip: Enter early morning when soft light filters through the ancient cypress grove. The main hall's interior wooden pillars and ceiling decorations are best photographed before 9am when tour groups arrive.

forbidden-city-core.name: Dongdan Gongyuan
forbidden-city-core.description: This neighborhood park near the bustling Dongdan shopping district offers locals a peaceful refuge with traditional exercise equipment, shaded pathways, and groups practicing tai chi at dawn. Visit early morning to experience authentic Beijing park culture as residents gather for group exercise, card games, and social activities.
forbidden-city-core.tip: Join the morning exercises between 6-8am when elderly locals lead free tai chi and fan dance sessions – they welcome visitors to participate.

forbidden-city-core.name: Changpuhe Park （West Gate 2）
forbidden-city-core.address: China, Bei Jing Shi, Dong Cheng Qu, Dong Chang An Jie, 东华门 邮政编码: 100006
forbidden-city-core.description: This linear riverside park runs alongside the ancient moat of the Forbidden City, featuring traditional Chinese gardens and stone bridges over the willow-lined Changpu River. The peaceful waterside promenade offers an authentic glimpse of local life as residents practice tai chi and play traditional instruments.
forbidden-city-core.tip: Enter through West Gate 2 in early morning to join locals doing calligraphy with water brushes on the pavement—a beautiful meditative practice you can try yourself.

forbidden-city-core.name: Wangfujing Pedestrian Street
forbidden-city-core.address: WC66+9GP, Wang Fu Jing Da Jie, Dong Cheng Qu, Bei Jing Shi, China, 100006
forbidden-city-core.summary: Restaurants, food stalls & stores line this bustling, well-known pedestrian street with sculptures.
forbidden-city-core.description: This historic commercial boulevard transforms into a sensory feast where century-old pharmacies stand alongside modern flagship stores, while the famous Snack Street offers skewered scorpions and exotic street food. The perfect blend of old Beijing shopping culture and contemporary retail therapy makes it a mandatory stop for understanding the capital's commercial heartbeat.
forbidden-city-core.tip: Visit after 7pm when the Snack Street becomes most atmospheric with illuminated stalls, but eat dinner beforehand—sample exotic items like starfish and grasshoppers for the experience, not sustenance.

forbidden-city-core.name: Drum Tower （Wenjin Street）
forbidden-city-core.address: W9JQ+P65, Jiao Chang Hu Tong, 西安门 Xi Cheng Qu, Bei Jing Shi, China, 100035
forbidden-city-core.description: This towering Ming dynasty drum tower once marked time for the entire capital with thunderous drum beats that echoed across the old city. Climb the steep wooden stairs to see the massive replica drums and enjoy sweeping views over Beijing's traditional hutong neighborhoods.
forbidden-city-core.tip: Visit around 9:30 AM or 2:30 PM to catch the traditional drum performance. The acoustics inside are powerful, so stand near the back if sensitive to loud sounds.

forbidden-city-core.name: Jiaolou
forbidden-city-core.description: These elegant watchtowers anchor each corner of the Forbidden City's walls with their distinctive three-tiered roofs and 72 ridges that seem to defy gravity. The northwestern tower offers the best exterior views framed by the palace moat, creating one of Beijing's most photographed classical compositions.
forbidden-city-core.tip: Visit the corner between the northwest tower and north gate at dawn when local photographers gather—they'll share the best angles and sometimes lend tripods.

forbidden-city-core.name: Olympic Park
forbidden-city-core.description: Home to the striking Bird's Nest stadium and Water Cube from the 2008 Olympics, this sprawling park showcases China's modern architectural ambition on a grand scale. The illuminated evening light shows transform these massive structures into glowing landmarks visible across northern Beijing.
forbidden-city-core.tip: Visit on weekday evenings after 7pm when locals gather for free dancing and tai chi sessions in the plaza areas, offering authentic cultural moments without tourist crowds.

hutong-drum-tower.name: Ditan Park
hutong-drum-tower.description: This historic imperial altar park, dating to 1530, features ancient cypress trees, traditional architecture, and the grand Square Altar where Ming and Qing emperors performed earth worship ceremonies. The park transforms during Chinese New Year into Beijing's liveliest temple fair, showcasing folk performances, traditional crafts, and regional snacks.
hutong-drum-tower.tip: Visit early morning to see locals practicing tai chi, ballroom dancing, and traditional music around the cypress groves—it's the most authentic glimpse of Beijing community life.

hutong-drum-tower.name: Lama Temple
hutong-drum-tower.address: 12 Yong He Gong Da Jie, 国子监 Dong Cheng Qu, China, 100011
hutong-drum-tower.summary: Ornate Tibetan Buddhist temple complex built in 1694 & featuring bronze statues & incense burners.
hutong-drum-tower.description: This Tibetan Buddhist monastery houses a 26-meter tall sandalwood Maitreya Buddha, one of the largest wooden sculptures in the world, carved from a single tree. The air is thick with juniper incense as red-robed monks chant prayers among ornate prayer wheels and intricate mandalas.
hutong-drum-tower.tip: Visit on weekday mornings before 10am to avoid tour groups. Bring small bills for incense offerings at each hall, and turn prayer wheels clockwise three times for good fortune.

hutong-drum-tower.name: Beijing Temple of Confucius
hutong-drum-tower.address: 15 Guo Zi Jian Jie, 国子监 Dong Cheng Qu, Bei Jing Shi, China, 100011
hutong-drum-tower.summary: 14th-century Confucian temple with grounds including monumental pavilions, gates & carvings.
hutong-drum-tower.description: This 700-year-old temple houses ancient stone tablets carved with the names of imperial exam graduates and features rows of gnarled cypress trees that create a serene courtyard atmosphere. The collection of 198 stone steles documenting Confucian classics represents one of China's most important historical archives.
hutong-drum-tower.tip: Visit on weekday mornings when local retirees practice calligraphy in the courtyards—some are happy to demonstrate traditional brush techniques and explain the inscriptions on the ancient tablets.

hutong-drum-tower.name: 地坛公园
hutong-drum-tower.address: China, Bei Jing Shi, Dong Cheng Qu, 蒋宅口安定门外大街XC38+4CQ 邮政编码: 100011
hutong-drum-tower.summary: A small 16th-century temple dedicated to the god of the earth in tranquil, grassy Ditan Park.
hutong-drum-tower.description: This 16th-century temple complex honors the god of earth with distinctive square altars and pathways that reflect ancient cosmology, set within expansive grounds filled with ancient cypress trees. The park transforms during Chinese New Year with one of Beijing's largest temple fairs, featuring traditional performances, folk crafts, and regional snacks.
hutong-drum-tower.tip: Visit the northwest corner early morning to watch elderly locals practice traditional Chinese arts like taichi, diabolo spinning, and opera singing in an authentic community setting.

hutong-drum-tower.name: 北京鼓楼和钟楼
hutong-drum-tower.address: China, Bei Jing Shi, Dong Cheng Qu, Andingmen, Zhong Lou Wan Hu Tong, 临9号 邮政编码: 100009
hutong-drum-tower.summary: Red watchtower reconstructed in 1420, offering views & performances with replica Ming dynasty drums.
hutong-drum-tower.description: These twin towers rise above Beijing's ancient hutongs, with the Drum Tower housing massive replica drums that thunder during traditional performances throughout the day. Climb the steep stairs for sweeping views across the old city's grey-tiled rooftops and the direct sight line to the Bell Tower.
hutong-drum-tower.tip: Visit at 9:30am, 11:30am, or 2pm to catch the dramatic drum performance. Buy a combo ticket covering both towers at the first one you enter to save money.

hutong-drum-tower.name: Hutong Tour
hutong-drum-tower.address: Around Qianhai Beiyan on Houhai Lake, Dongcheng district, W9QR+X95西城区 China, 100035
hutong-drum-tower.description: Wind through Beijing's ancient alleyways on foot or by rickshaw, passing courtyard homes, local residents playing mahjong, and tiny neighborhood shops that have served the same families for generations. Experience authentic old Beijing life that survives amid the modern city's rapid transformation.
hutong-drum-tower.tip: Visit in late afternoon when residents gather outside their courtyard gates to chat, cook, and play chess—the best time to observe genuine neighborhood life.

hutong-drum-tower.name: Drum Tower
hutong-drum-tower.address: China, Bei Jing Shi, Dong Cheng Qu, 国子监 15 邮政编码: 100011
hutong-drum-tower.description: This imposing 15th-century tower once marked time for the entire imperial city with thundering drum performances that echoed across Beijing's rooftops. Climb the steep wooden stairs to stand among replica ceremonial drums and gain sweeping views over the grey-tiled hutong neighborhoods stretching toward the Forbidden City.
hutong-drum-tower.tip: Visit during one of the drum performance times (usually 9:30am, 11:30am, 1:30pm, 3:30pm, 4:45pm) to experience the powerful reverberations that once regulated daily life in ancient Beijing.

hutong-drum-tower.name: Drum and Bell
hutong-drum-tower.address: 41 Zhong Lou Wan Hu Tong, Dong Cheng Qu, Bei Jing Shi, China, 100035
hutong-drum-tower.description: These twin Ming dynasty towers once regulated Beijing's daily rhythms, with massive drums and bells marking time across the ancient capital. Climb the steep wooden stairs to reach the enormous drums and enjoy sweeping views over the surrounding hutong neighborhoods.
hutong-drum-tower.tip: Visit at 9:30am or 11:30am for the traditional drum performance on weekdays. The acoustics are impressive inside the wooden chamber during the synchronized drumming display.

hutong-drum-tower.name: Shuangxiu Park （North Gate）
hutong-drum-tower.address: 20 Bei San Huan Zhong Lu, Xi Cheng Qu, Bei Jing Shi, China, 100120
hutong-drum-tower.description: This peaceful neighborhood park near the Drum Tower features traditional Chinese gardens with winding paths, willow-lined ponds, and pavilions where locals practice tai chi and traditional music. The serene Japanese garden section offers a quiet escape from Beijing's busy hutong streets.
hutong-drum-tower.tip: Visit early morning to see locals practicing calligraphy with water brushes on the pavement and join free tai chi sessions near the main pond.

temple-of-heaven.name: 潘家园古玩市场
temple-of-heaven.summary: Bustling market boasting lots of stalls selling jewelry, antiques, household decor & crafts.
temple-of-heaven.description: This sprawling weekend market stretches across hundreds of stalls where vendors hawk everything from Mao-era propaganda posters to jade pendants, Tibetan prayer wheels, and dubious 'Ming dynasty' vases. The real draw is the theatrical haggling culture where prices can drop by 70% if you know how to negotiate.
temple-of-heaven.tip: Arrive before 7am on weekends when serious collectors descend and vendors display their best items. Bring cash and assume most antiques are reproductions unless you're an expert.

temple-of-heaven.name: 祈年门
temple-of-heaven.address: China, Bei Jing Shi, Dong Cheng Qu, 天坛路天坛路天坛公园内 邮政编码: 100061
temple-of-heaven.description: This grand triple-arched gate serves as the ceremonial entrance to the Temple of Heaven's inner sanctum, where Ming and Qing emperors once passed through for harvest rituals. The intricate vermillion woodwork and traditional glazed roof tiles frame perfect views of the Hall of Prayer for Good Harvests beyond.
temple-of-heaven.tip: Visit early morning when golden light streams through the archways and locals practice tai chi nearby. The gate is less crowded than the main hall, allowing unobstructed photos.

temple-of-heaven.name: 祈年殿
temple-of-heaven.address: China, Bei Jing Shi, Dong Cheng Qu, 天坛路天坛路天坛公园内 邮政编码: 100061
temple-of-heaven.description: This triple-gabled circular hall, built entirely without nails, features 28 massive pillars symbolizing celestial cycles and showcases Ming Dynasty woodworking mastery. The brilliant blue-tiled roof and elaborate interior carvings represent the pinnacle of imperial sacrificial architecture where emperors once prayed for good harvests.
temple-of-heaven.tip: Visit right at opening time to photograph the hall without crowds, and walk the raised marble pathway emperors used—tourists often miss that only the emperor could walk its center strip.

temple-of-heaven.name: Temple of Heaven Park （South Gate）
temple-of-heaven.address: China, Bei Jing Shi, Dong Cheng Qu, Jing Tai Lu, 天坛路甲1号 邮政编码: 100061
temple-of-heaven.description: The southern entrance to Beijing's Temple of Heaven complex provides direct access to the Circular Mound Altar where Ming and Qing emperors performed elaborate winter solstice ceremonies. This entry point offers the most strategic route to explore the park's ancient imperial worship grounds and century-old cypress groves with fewer crowds than the main gates.
temple-of-heaven.tip: Enter early at 6 AM when elderly locals practice tai chi and traditional music around the ancient trees—you can join free lessons near the Echo Wall.

temple-of-heaven.name: Happy Valley Beijing
temple-of-heaven.address: China, Bei Jing Shi, Chao Yang Qu, 北京市朝阳区东四环小武基北路 邮政编码: 100122
temple-of-heaven.summary: Fun park with themed areas that include roller coasters & other thrill rides, plus a train & shows.
temple-of-heaven.description: This sprawling amusement park features six themed zones with over 40 rides, from gravity-defying roller coasters like the Crystal Wing to family-friendly carousels and water flumes. The park's combination of intense thrill rides and elaborate evening shows with acrobatics and fireworks makes it Beijing's premier destination for adrenaline seekers.
temple-of-heaven.tip: Visit on weekdays during school terms to avoid massive crowds. Download the park's official app beforehand to check real-time wait times and book fast passes for popular rides.

temple-of-heaven.name: Longtan Lake Park
temple-of-heaven.address: 8 Long Tan Lu, Dong Cheng Qu, Bei Jing Shi, China, 100062
temple-of-heaven.summary: Public park developed around a large lake, with many pavilions and bridges dotting its perimeter.
temple-of-heaven.description: This expansive lake park features traditional Chinese pavilions and zigzagging bridges that reflect beautifully in the calm waters, creating perfect symmetry for photography enthusiasts. Local residents gather here for morning tai chi sessions, paddle boats, and peaceful lakeside walks away from Beijing's urban rush.
temple-of-heaven.tip: Visit the northeast corner early morning to join free community tai chi classes led by local masters, or rent a paddle boat for 30-40 RMB per hour to explore the lake's quieter coves.

summer-palace.name: Yuanmingyuan Park
summer-palace.summary: Park featuring colorful gardens & ruins dating from 1707 & burned during Second Opium War of 1860.
summer-palace.description: This sprawling imperial garden showcases haunting European-style palace ruins alongside serene lakes and reconstructed Chinese pavilions, remnants of the 1860 destruction during the Second Opium War. The juxtaposition of crumbling baroque columns against traditional gardens creates a powerful, photogenic reminder of China's tumultuous past.
summer-palace.tip: Visit the Western Mansions ruins section early morning for soft light photography and fewer crowds. Rent a bicycle at the entrance to cover the vast 350-hectare grounds efficiently.

summer-palace.name: Baiwangshan Forest Park
summer-palace.summary: Sizable park with maple trees & a mountaintop pavilion, plus rock-climbing routes & hiking trails.
summer-palace.description: This forested mountain park northwest of Beijing features vibrant maple groves that blaze red in autumn, rock-climbing walls built into natural cliff faces, and a historic pavilion perched at the 210-meter summit. The combination of managed hiking trails, climbing routes, and expansive views toward the Western Hills makes it a favorite weekend escape for active Beijing locals.
summer-palace.tip: Arrive early on autumn weekends to avoid crowds and secure parking. The north slope trail is less crowded than the main route and offers better maple viewing from late October through early November.

summer-palace.name: Shihuadong Scenic Area
summer-palace.address: QXW3+R5G, Shi Hua Dong Lu, Fang Shan Qu, Bei Jing Shi, China, 102415
summer-palace.description: This limestone cave system 50km southwest of Beijing features otherworldly rock formations, stalactites, and underground rivers formed over 500 million years. The multi-level caverns maintain a constant cool temperature year-round, offering a natural escape from Beijing's summer heat.
summer-palace.tip: Wear sturdy shoes with good grip as the cave paths can be slippery, and bring a light jacket since temperatures inside stay around 13°C even in summer.

summer-palace.name: 十七孔桥
summer-palace.address: X7RH+29X, Kun Ming Hu Lu, Hai Dian Qu, Bei Jing Shi, China, 100080
summer-palace.description: This 150-meter Qing Dynasty bridge features seventeen graceful arches reflected in Kunming Lake, with 544 uniquely carved stone lions adorning its balustrades. The late afternoon light transforms the arches into golden frames, creating one of Beijing's most photographed water views.
summer-palace.tip: Visit during winter sunset when the sun aligns perfectly through all seventeen arches, a phenomenon that occurs only a few days each year around winter solstice.

summer-palace.name: The Ruins of Yuanmingyuan （Southeast Gate）
summer-palace.chinese: 圆明园遗址公园（东南门）
summer-palace.address: 2847+GWJ, Zhong Guan Cun Bei Da Jie, 圆明园 Hai Dian Qu, Bei Jing Shi, China, 100084
summer-palace.description: These haunting ruins of the former imperial summer palace reveal crumbling Western-style stone facades and archways, stark reminders of its destruction in 1860. The juxtaposition of decaying European baroque architecture against traditional Chinese garden landscapes creates a powerful historical narrative rarely seen elsewhere in Beijing.
summer-palace.tip: Enter through the Southeast Gate to reach the Western Mansions ruins directly, avoiding the long walk from the main entrance and saving at least 30 minutes.

summer-palace.name: The Summer Palace Wenchang Gallery
summer-palace.address: X7WJ+PH6, Kun Ming Hu Dong Lu, Hai Dian Qu, Bei Jing Shi, China, 100000
summer-palace.description: This historic gallery within the Summer Palace complex once served as Emperor Guangxu's study and now displays precious imperial artifacts, calligraphy, and paintings. Visit to see rare bronze collections and scholarly treasures that reveal the intellectual pursuits of Qing Dynasty royalty in an intimate courtyard setting.
summer-palace.tip: Visit during weekday mornings when tour groups focus on the main palace areas, allowing you to appreciate the calligraphy collections and bronze exhibits in relative quiet.

summer-palace.name: The Summer Palace Water Tea House
summer-palace.description: This traditional teahouse sits on stilts over Kunming Lake within the Summer Palace grounds, offering authentic Chinese tea ceremonies with views across the water to Longevity Hill. The serene waterside setting provides a peaceful escape where you can experience classical Beijing tea culture in an imperial garden environment.
summer-palace.tip: Visit mid-morning on weekdays to avoid tour groups and secure a lakeside table. Ask for the jasmine tea grown in nearby Fragrant Hills for the most local experience.

summer-palace.name: Long Corridor
summer-palace.summary: Built in 1750, this 728-m.-long passageway in the Summer Palace features 14,000+ paintings.
summer-palace.description: This 728-meter covered walkway features over 14,000 hand-painted scenes from Chinese literature, mythology, and landscapes on its beams and ceilings, making it the longest decorated corridor in classical Chinese gardens. Walking beneath centuries-old artwork while protected from sun and rain offers an immersive journey through traditional Chinese storytelling and craftsmanship.
summer-palace.tip: Visit early morning when tour groups haven't arrived to photograph the paintings without crowds. Look up frequently—the most intricate artwork is on the ceiling beams, not the side panels.

summer-palace.name: Forest Park
summer-palace.description: This expansive green space near the Summer Palace offers tree-lined pathways, tranquil ponds, and wide lawns popular with locals practicing tai chi and flying kites. The peaceful atmosphere and uncrowded trails make it perfect for escaping Beijing's urban intensity without leaving the city.
summer-palace.tip: Visit early morning to join locals doing their exercise routines and observe authentic Beijing park culture before the afternoon heat sets in.

summer-palace.name: Beijing Botanical Garden
summer-palace.address: China, Bei Jing Shi, Hai Dian Qu, 香山路X6X5+MJC 邮政编码: 100093
summer-palace.summary: Sizable botanical park with walking trails, a greenhouse & diverse plant & flower species.
summer-palace.description: This expansive botanical garden showcases over 6,000 plant species across themed zones, from medicinal herb gardens to a spectacular peach blossom valley. The conservatory houses rare tropical and desert plants under a massive glass dome, offering a climate-controlled escape year-round.
summer-palace.tip: Visit the Cao Xueqin Memorial in the northwest corner, a tranquil traditional garden often missed by tourists that inspired scenes from Dream of the Red Chamber.

standalone.name: Beijing Badaling National Forest Park
standalone.address: China, Bei Jing Shi, Yan Qing Qu, G6京藏高速八达岭 邮政编码: 102112
standalone.description: This forested mountain park surrounds the famous Great Wall at Badaling, offering colorful autumn foliage, spring blossoms, and hiking trails through pine forests away from the wall crowds. Visit for the dramatic red leaf displays in October that blanket the hillsides in fiery crimson.
standalone.tip: Enter from the lesser-known south entrance to access scenic cable car rides and forest trails with Great Wall views minus the tourist masses at Badaling proper.

standalone.name: Great Wall of China
standalone.address: Huairou District, China, 101406
standalone.summary: Ancient world wonder that winds for more than 8,850 km across China, with towers & walkways.
standalone.description: This ancient fortification snakes dramatically across mountain ridges, with watchtowers offering sweeping views of rugged terrain that shifts from lush green valleys to golden autumn slopes. Walking atop the weathered stone ramparts connects you to over 2,000 years of Chinese imperial defense history in the most tangible way possible.
standalone.tip: Visit Mutianyu section instead of overcrowded Badaling—it has a cable car up and toboggan slide down, plus far fewer tour groups clogging the walkways.

standalone.name: 丁香谷
standalone.address: 82MJ+C87, Yanqing District, Beijing, China, 102112
standalone.description: This tranquil valley comes alive each spring when thousands of lilac bushes burst into fragrant purple and white blooms, creating a naturally perfumed escape from urban Beijing. The peaceful walking trails through dense lilac groves offer a rare opportunity to experience nature's aromatherapy while surrounded by colorful blossoms.
standalone.tip: Visit in early May during peak lilac season on weekday mornings to avoid crowds and experience the strongest fragrance when morning dew intensifies the floral scent.

standalone.name: Yeyahu Wetland Park
standalone.address: China, Bei Jing Shi, Yan Qing Qu, 八达岭长城脚下的官厅湖畔 邮政编码: 102112
standalone.description: This expansive wetland sanctuary in Beijing's northern suburbs shelters over 300 bird species, including rare black storks and oriental white storks during migration seasons. The elevated boardwalks and observation towers offer exceptional opportunities for birdwatching and photography across serene marshlands and open water.
standalone.tip: Visit during spring (March-May) or autumn (September-November) migration periods for the best birdwatching. Bring binoculars and arrive early morning when birds are most active.

standalone.name: Ming Dynasty Tombs
standalone.address: 764F+3C3, Chang Chi Lu, Chang Ping Qu, China, 102200
standalone.summary: Immense, ancient complex including the tombs of 13 Ming Dynasty emperors plus pathways & statues.
standalone.description: Thirteen emperors of the Ming Dynasty rest in this vast necropolis, where massive stone archways, ceremonial pathways lined with mythical animal statues, and ornate burial chambers reveal imperial burial traditions spanning two centuries. The Sacred Way, a kilometer-long avenue flanked by 24 giant stone guardians, offers an atmospheric journey into China's imperial past.
standalone.tip: Visit Dingling Tomb first—it's the only one with accessible underground burial chambers where you can see the actual vaults where Emperor Wanli and his empresses were entombed.

standalone.name: Beijing Ming Imperial Palace
standalone.address: China, Bei Jing Shi, Chang Ping Qu, G6辅路66F7+M7M 邮政编码: 102200
standalone.description: This imperial palace complex showcases Ming Dynasty architecture with its red walls, golden-tiled roofs, and ceremonial halls that once housed emperors and their courts. Visit to walk through centuries of Chinese imperial history where political power and cultural traditions shaped the nation.
standalone.tip: Arrive right at opening time to experience the palace courtyards in relative quiet before tour groups flood in around mid-morning.

olympic-park.name: Dayunhe Forest Park
olympic-park.description: This expansive forest park stretches along the historic Grand Canal, offering tree-lined riverside paths, wetlands, and traditional Chinese gardens that showcase Beijing's ecological restoration efforts. The peaceful waterfront trails provide a refreshing escape from urban density, perfect for cycling or leisurely walks beside one of China's most important historical waterways.
olympic-park.tip: Rent a bicycle at the north entrance to cover more ground—the park spans over 10 kilometers along the canal, and cycling lets you explore hidden lakeside spots most visitors miss.

olympic-park.name: Jicui
olympic-park.address: W9FQ+9MH西安门 Xicheng District, Beijing, China, 100034
olympic-park.description: This scenic spot in Olympic Park features carefully landscaped gardens with traditional Chinese design elements and peaceful water features. The tranquil green space offers a refreshing escape from Beijing's urban intensity, perfect for a leisurely stroll through manicured pathways.
olympic-park.tip: Visit early morning to see local residents practicing tai chi and traditional exercises—join in for an authentic cultural experience that costs nothing.

olympic-park.name: Drum Tower
olympic-park.description: This 15th-century bell and drum tower once marked time for imperial Beijing with thunderous drumbeats that echoed across the city. Climb the steep wooden stairs to see massive original drums and enjoy panoramic views over the hutongs stretching toward the Forbidden City.
olympic-park.tip: Visit at opening time to catch the traditional drum performance at 9:30am, which only runs a few times daily and gets crowded quickly.

olympic-park.name: 砖塔胡同
olympic-park.address: Zhuan Ta Hu Tong, Xi Cheng Qu, Bei Jing Shi, China, 100033
olympic-park.description: This narrow alley houses Beijing's oldest surviving Yuan Dynasty pagoda, a 16-meter brick tower dating back to the 13th century that gives the hutong its name. Walking through the quiet residential lanes offers an authentic glimpse of old Beijing neighborhoods surrounding this ancient architectural relic.
olympic-park.tip: Visit early morning when elderly residents practice tai chi near the pagoda base. The tower itself isn't accessible inside, but the surrounding hutong courtyards reveal traditional siheyuan architecture.

olympic-park.name: National Stadium
olympic-park.address: 1 Guo Jia Ti Yu Chang Nan Lu, Chao Yang Qu, China, 100107
olympic-park.summary: Iconic stadium known as the Bird's Nest built for the Olympics & now hosting concerts & sports.
olympic-park.description: The striking steel lattice structure nicknamed the Bird's Nest hosted the 2008 Olympics opening ceremony and remains one of the world's most distinctive modern architectural achievements. Walk through the stadium's interior to appreciate its intricate metalwork design up close and explore the Olympic legacy exhibitions inside.
olympic-park.tip: Visit during late afternoon on weekdays to avoid tour groups. The rooftop viewing platform offers unique perspectives of the structure itself, though access depends on events scheduled that day.

olympic-park.name: Olympic Forest Park （South Gate）
olympic-park.chinese: 奥林匹克森林公园（南门）
olympic-park.address: 296R+HXF, Ke Hui Lu, Chao Yang Qu, Bei Jing Shi, China, 100107
olympic-park.description: This expansive green space, built for the 2008 Beijing Olympics, features wetlands, wildflower meadows, and tree-lined paths stretching across 680 hectares. The southern section offers easy access to the iconic Olympic Tower and lake views perfect for leisurely strolls or morning exercise.
olympic-park.tip: Enter through the south gate early morning to join locals practicing tai chi by the lake, then follow the eastern path to find the quieter cherry blossom grove.

olympic-park.name: 北京奥林匹克塔
olympic-park.address: China, 北京市朝阳区安贞294Q+Q97 邮政编码: 100107
olympic-park.description: Five interconnected towers rising 246 meters offer revolving observation decks with panoramic views of the Olympic Park, Bird's Nest, and Water Cube. The outdoor sky corridor and nightly LED light show transform the structure into a glowing beacon visible across northern Beijing.
olympic-park.tip: Visit at sunset to catch daytime views and stay for the evening light show. Book tickets online for express entry and skip the ground floor queues.

olympic-park.name: Olympic Forest Park （North Gate）
olympic-park.chinese: 奥林匹克森林公园（北门）
olympic-park.address: China, Bei Jing Shi, Chao Yang Qu, S50北五环辅路2CC2+G25 邮政编码: 100101
olympic-park.description: Beijing's largest urban park sprawls across 680 hectares with expansive wetlands, tree-lined trails, and views of the Olympic stadiums to the south. The northern section offers quieter walking paths away from tourist crowds, perfect for morning jogging or leisurely afternoon picnics among locals.
olympic-park.tip: Enter through the North Gate early morning to see local retirees practicing tai chi by the lake, then follow the western trail to spot wild birds at the wetland observation deck.

olympic-park.name: Olympic Forest Park （Northwest Gate）
olympic-park.chinese: 奥林匹克森林公园（西北门）
olympic-park.address: China, Bei Jing Shi, Chao Yang Qu, 滨河路科荟路33号奥林匹克森林公园内 邮政编码: 102218
olympic-park.description: This expansive green lung north of the Olympic venues features wetlands, wildflower meadows, and tree-lined paths that offer a peaceful escape from urban Beijing. The park's southern lake with views toward the iconic Bird's Nest stadium provides the perfect blend of nature and modern architecture.
olympic-park.tip: Enter through the Northwest Gate early morning to access the quieter northern section first, where locals practice tai chi by the wetlands and you'll avoid tour groups concentrated near the southern Olympic area.

olympic-park.name: Olympic Forest Park North Park （East Gate）
olympic-park.chinese: 奥林匹克森林公园北园（东门）
olympic-park.address: 25 An Li Lu, Chao Yang Qu, Bei Jing Shi, China, 102218
olympic-park.description: This expansive green sanctuary features wetlands, meadows, and tree-lined paths that stretch for kilometers beyond the Olympic complex. The park offers exceptional space for morning tai chi, cycling, and picnicking away from Beijing's urban intensity.
olympic-park.tip: Visit the wetland area near the north section early morning for birdwatching—locals bring binoculars to spot migratory species. The eastern trails are less crowded than western routes.

olympic-park.name: 奥林匹克森林公园北园
olympic-park.chinese: 奥林匹克森林公园北园
olympic-park.address: China, Bei Jing Shi, Chao Yang Qu, 仰山桥 邮政编码: 102218
olympic-park.description: The northern section of Olympic Forest Park features expansive wildflower meadows, wetlands with migratory birds, and forested trails that feel worlds away from Beijing's urban core. Visit for peaceful morning walks or jogs on well-maintained paths that showcase changing seasons through native plantings and restored natural habitats.
olympic-park.tip: Enter from the north gate early morning to spot herons and egrets in the wetlands before crowds arrive. Locals recommend the elevated wooden boardwalk for best bird-watching views.

olympic-park.name: Olympic Park （Northwest Gate）
olympic-park.address: 292V+Q5W, Da Tun Lu, Olympic Village, Chao Yang Qu, Bei Jing Shi, China, 100107
olympic-park.description: This gateway accesses Beijing's Olympic legacy including the Bird's Nest and Water Cube, set within expansive parklands featuring contemporary sculptures and tree-lined promenades. The grand scale and architectural innovation of the 2008 Olympic venues create an unforgettable impression of modern China's ambitions.
olympic-park.tip: Enter through this northwest gate early morning to photograph the Olympic venues with soft light and fewer crowds, then rent a bicycle to efficiently cover the vast park area.

olympic-park.name: National Zoological Museum of China
olympic-park.address: 293J+2JH, Da Tun Lu, Olympic Village, Chao Yang Qu, Bei Jing Shi, China, 100107
olympic-park.description: This comprehensive zoological museum houses over 600,000 specimens spanning China's diverse wildlife, from giant pandas to rare insects, displayed across modern exhibition halls. The vivid dioramas recreating natural habitats with taxidermied animals offer an educational journey through China's ecosystems without traveling thousands of kilometers.
olympic-park.tip: Visit the butterfly and insect hall on the fourth floor first as it's less crowded in mornings and features stunning specimens that most visitors skip in favor of the mammal exhibits.

xicheng-zoo.name: Lianhuachi Gongyuan
xicheng-zoo.description: This historic park centers around a 1,000-year-old lotus pond that once supplied the Forbidden City with water, featuring traditional pavilions and willow-lined paths. Visit during summer months when thousands of lotus blossoms transform the pond into a sea of pink and white flowers.
xicheng-zoo.tip: Arrive early morning around 6-7am to watch local retirees practicing tai chi by the water and enjoy the lotus flowers before tour groups arrive.

xicheng-zoo.name: 玉渊潭公园
xicheng-zoo.address: China, Bei Jing Shi, Hai Dian Qu, 玉渊潭南路 邮政编码: 100048
xicheng-zoo.summary: Peaceful haven with expanses of water & recreational facilities, plus a cherry garden & monuments.
xicheng-zoo.description: This expansive lakeside park transforms into a sea of pink each spring when over 3,000 cherry blossom trees bloom across its waters and pathways. The combination of traditional Chinese pavilions reflecting in tranquil waters with seasonal blossoms creates an unparalleled urban escape.
xicheng-zoo.tip: Visit the Sakura Garden's eastern shore at dawn during late March to early April for cherry blossoms without the crowds, and bring a thermos for a peaceful lakeside tea moment.

xicheng-zoo.name: Ritan Gongyuan
xicheng-zoo.description: This Ming dynasty altar park centers around the historic Sun Altar where emperors once performed rituals at dawn to honor the sun. The peaceful tree-lined paths and traditional architecture offer a genuine glimpse into imperial Beijing away from tourist crowds.
xicheng-zoo.tip: Visit the northeast corner to find locals practicing tai chi and traditional music every morning around 6-7am—a genuine cultural experience rarely seen by tourists.

xicheng-zoo.name: Beijing Dongyue Temple
xicheng-zoo.address: 141 Chao Yang Men Wai Da Jie, Chaowai Subdistrict, Chao Yang Qu, Bei Jing Shi, China, 100020
xicheng-zoo.summary: Centuries-old Taoist sacred site with many ghoulish sculptures dedicated to the afterlife.
xicheng-zoo.description: This 700-year-old Taoist temple houses eerie departments of the underworld, with vivid statues depicting demons, judgment scenes, and the bureaucracy of the afterlife. The macabre Hall of Seventy-Six Departments offers a haunting glimpse into Chinese folk beliefs about death and retribution.
xicheng-zoo.tip: Visit the Department of Wandering Ghosts in the western courtyard—locals leave offerings here for ancestors who died far from home, creating an atmospheric and deeply personal experience.

xicheng-zoo.name: Temple of the Sun
xicheng-zoo.summary: Remains of a 16th-century temple in landscaped surrounds with trees, gardens & a lake.
xicheng-zoo.description: This 16th-century imperial altar sits within peaceful gardens where emperors once worshipped the sun during spring equinoxes. The tranquil lake and ancient cypress trees create a serene escape from Beijing's urban hustle, perfect for contemplative walks through Ming Dynasty history.
xicheng-zoo.tip: Visit during early morning to see local tai chi practitioners gathering near the altar platform, offering an authentic glimpse of neighborhood life that tour groups miss.

xicheng-zoo.name: Wanshou Park
xicheng-zoo.address: 1 Bai Zhi Fang Dong Jie, Xi Cheng Qu, Bei Jing Shi, China, 100053
xicheng-zoo.description: This tranquil neighborhood park in Xicheng District features traditional Chinese pavilions, winding pathways, and a serene pond where local residents practice tai chi and play traditional instruments. Perfect for observing authentic Beijing daily life away from tourist crowds.
xicheng-zoo.tip: Visit early morning around 6-7 AM to see elderly locals doing tai chi, singing Peking opera, and playing erhu by the water—a genuine slice of Beijing community culture.

xicheng-zoo.name: Drum Tower
xicheng-zoo.address: WCFV+HQC, Chaowai Subdistrict, Chaoyang, Beijing, China, 100020
xicheng-zoo.description: This 15th-century tower once marked time for the entire imperial city with massive drums that thundered across Beijing's rooftops. Climb the steep wooden stairs to reach the original cowhide drums and panoramic views over the hutongs connecting to nearby Bell Tower.
xicheng-zoo.tip: Visit around 9:30am or 2:30pm for the traditional drum performance. The upper floor gets crowded fast, so arrive 15 minutes early to secure a good viewing spot.

xicheng-zoo.name: Olympic park
xicheng-zoo.address: China, Bei Jing Shi, Xi Cheng Qu, 复兴门南大街甲2号 邮政编码: 100032
xicheng-zoo.description: Home to the striking Bird's Nest stadium and Water Cube from the 2008 Olympics, this expansive park blends modern architecture with landscaped gardens and wide promenades. The illuminated venues at dusk create a spectacular urban skyline that showcases Beijing's contemporary face.
xicheng-zoo.tip: Visit on weekday evenings to avoid crowds and catch the free light show on the Water Cube's exterior, which cycles through mesmerizing color patterns every few minutes.

xicheng-zoo.name: Wukesong Olympic Cultural Park （North Gate）
xicheng-zoo.chinese: 五棵松奥林匹克文化公园（北门）
xicheng-zoo.address: W77F+3VR, Yu Yuan Tan Nan Lu, 五棵松 Hai Dian Qu, Bei Jing Shi, China, 100039
xicheng-zoo.description: This modern urban park was built for the 2008 Beijing Olympics and features wide green spaces, contemporary sculptures, and well-maintained walking paths surrounding the famous Wukesong Arena. It's an ideal spot for a relaxing stroll away from Beijing's crowded tourist centers, where locals gather for morning exercises and evening leisure.
xicheng-zoo.tip: Visit early morning to join locals practicing tai chi and traditional dance near the north entrance, or come evening when the Wukesong Arena lights up and the park transforms into a lively gathering spot.

xicheng-zoo.name: Beijing Zoo
xicheng-zoo.address: 137 Xi Zhi Men Wai Da Jie, Xi Cheng Qu, Bei Jing Shi, China, 100044
xicheng-zoo.summary: Venerable zoo, founded in 1906, with traditional landscaping & native animals such as pandas.
xicheng-zoo.description: One of China's oldest zoos spans 220 acres with classical Chinese gardens, lotus ponds, and over 14,500 animals including the famous giant pandas in their dedicated exhibition hall. The resident giant pandas are the main draw, but arrive early to catch feeding times when they're most active and playful.
xicheng-zoo.tip: Visit the panda house right when the zoo opens at 7:30am to avoid crowds and see pandas during their morning feeding session when they're most energetic.

xicheng-zoo.name: Paleozoological Museum of China
xicheng-zoo.address: 142 Xi Wai Da Jie, Xi Cheng Qu, Bei Jing Shi, China, 100044
xicheng-zoo.summary: Compact 3-story, kid-friendly museum featuring dinosaur skeletons, fossils & a gift shop.
xicheng-zoo.description: This compact three-story museum houses an impressive collection of dinosaur skeletons and ancient fossils that bring China's prehistoric past to life through well-curated displays. The interactive exhibits and life-sized dinosaur reconstructions make complex paleontology accessible and engaging for visitors of all ages.
xicheng-zoo.tip: Visit on weekday mornings to avoid school groups. The third floor has the most spectacular specimens including complete dinosaur skeletons, so start from the top and work your way down.

xicheng-zoo.name: Gibbon
xicheng-zoo.address: China, Bei Jing Shi, Xi Cheng Qu, Xi Zhi Men Wai Da Jie, 137号北京动物园 邮政编码: 100044
xicheng-zoo.description: Watch these acrobatic primates swing gracefully through their habitat at Beijing Zoo's primate section, their distinctive long arms propelling them in fluid arcs. The gibbons' morning songs—melodious calls that echo through the enclosure—create an unexpectedly haunting soundtrack to your visit.
xicheng-zoo.tip: Visit during feeding time around 9:30 AM when keepers encourage natural foraging behaviors and the gibbons are most active and vocal.

xicheng-zoo.name: 儿童动物园
xicheng-zoo.address: China, Bei Jing Shi, Xi Cheng Qu, 北京动物园 邮政编码: 100044
xicheng-zoo.description: This dedicated children's zoo within Beijing's Zoo complex features smaller, domesticated animals including rabbits, guinea pigs, and young farm animals that children can observe up close in safe enclosures. Perfect for toddlers and young children to experience gentle animal encounters in a manageable, non-overwhelming environment.
xicheng-zoo.tip: Visit during weekday mornings when local kindergarten groups aren't scheduled, allowing your children more space and closer viewing of the animals without crowds.

xicheng-zoo.name: 紫竹院公园
xicheng-zoo.address: China, Bei Jing Shi, Hai Dian Qu, 北下关中关村南大街35号 邮政编码: 100089
xicheng-zoo.summary: City park made up chiefly of 3 interconnected lakes & walking trails winding between them.
xicheng-zoo.description: This lakeside park features three serene interconnected waters framed by groves of purple bamboo that rustle in the breeze, creating natural corridors popular with local tai chi practitioners and paddleboaters. The bamboo gardens offer a rare taste of southern Chinese landscape design within Beijing's urban core.
xicheng-zoo.tip: Visit the northern lake area early morning to see elderly musicians gather for impromptu traditional instrument sessions near the bamboo groves—it's a weekly ritual locals have maintained for decades.

xicheng-zoo.name: Beijing Zoo
xicheng-zoo.description: One of China's oldest zoos houses over 14,500 animals including the world-famous giant pandas in spacious traditional Chinese garden settings. The panda hall remains the star attraction where visitors can observe these beloved creatures lounging, munching bamboo, and occasionally tumbling about in their enclosures.
xicheng-zoo.tip: Arrive right at opening time to see the pandas at their most active during morning feeding. The crowds swell dramatically after 10am, especially on weekends.

xicheng-zoo.name: Milu
xicheng-zoo.address: China, Bei Jing Shi, Xi Cheng Qu, Xi Zhi Men Wai Da Jie, 137号北京动物园 邮政编码: 100044
xicheng-zoo.description: This specialized deer park within Beijing Zoo showcases China's native Père David's deer, once extinct in the wild and brought back through international conservation efforts. The semi-wild enclosure allows close observation of these unique antlered creatures with distinctive splayed hooves adapted for wetland habitats.
xicheng-zoo.tip: Visit during late morning feeding times when the milu are most active and approach the viewing areas. The deer are significantly more visible in cooler months.

xicheng-zoo.name: Beijing Zoo （North Gate）
xicheng-zoo.address: China, Bei Jing Shi, Hai Dian Qu, Qi Xiang Lu, 高粱桥斜街乙18号 邮政编码: 100082
xicheng-zoo.description: Home to over 14,500 animals including China's beloved giant pandas, this sprawling zoo combines traditional Chinese gardens with modern exhibits across 220 acres. The panda hall draws massive crowds, offering rare close-up views of these gentle giants munching bamboo in spacious, naturalistic enclosures.
xicheng-zoo.tip: Enter through the North Gate to avoid tour group congestion and head straight to the panda house when it opens—pandas are most active during morning feeding time around 9-10am.

west-lake-core.name: Hangzhou West Lake Scenery Spot
west-lake-core.address: 64RV+2XQ, Xihu, Hangzhou, Zhejiang, China, 310007
west-lake-core.description: This UNESCO World Heritage Site features a vast freshwater lake surrounded by ancient pagodas, arched bridges, and willow-lined causeways that have inspired Chinese poets for centuries. The Su Causeway walk at dawn offers mirror-like reflections of surrounding hills and temples emerging from morning mist.
west-lake-core.tip: Rent a public bicycle from any station around the lake to cover more ground efficiently—the complete circuit is about 15 kilometers and bike paths are well-maintained and clearly marked.

west-lake-core.name: 苏堤
west-lake-core.address: China, Zhe Jiang Sheng, Hang Zhou Shi, Xi Hu Qu, 南山路 邮政编码: 310007
west-lake-core.summary: Celebrated lake causeway featuring a paved walking path lined with willow trees & stunning views.
