tianya-haijiao.description: Ancient Taoist sculptures emerge from massive weathered boulders along a dramatic coastline where subtropical rainforest meets the sea. The natural rock formations and centuries-old carvings create a mystical landscape that has attracted spiritual seekers for over 800 years.
tianya-haijiao.tip: Visit the South Sea Dragon King statue area during late afternoon when tour groups have left and golden light illuminates the carved stone faces against the turquoise water.

tianya-haijiao.name: Sanya Bay Sports Park （South Gate）
tianya-haijiao.address: 7CR2+F87, Xin Cheng Lu, Tian Ya Qu, San Ya Shi, Hai Nan Sheng, China, 572023
tianya-haijiao.description: This waterfront sports park features open-air fitness equipment, basketball courts, and a running track with direct views of Sanya Bay's coastline. Perfect for joining local morning exercise groups or enjoying an evening jog as the sea breeze cools the tropical heat.
tianya-haijiao.tip: Visit around 6-7 AM to see locals practicing tai chi and traditional dance routines—you're welcome to join in for a uniquely interactive cultural experience.

tianya-haijiao.name: A Pillar Piercing into the Southern Sky
tianya-haijiao.description: This towering natural rock pillar rises dramatically from the turquoise waters near Tianya Haijiao, famously featured on China's 2 yuan banknote. The unique geological formation and its celebrity status as a currency landmark make it one of Sanya's most photographed natural monuments.
tianya-haijiao.tip: Visit during low tide in late afternoon when the water recedes enough to walk closer to the pillar base for unique photo angles without the midday cruise ship crowds.

tianya-haijiao.name: Tianya-haijiao Scenic Area Ticket Office
tianya-haijiao.address: China, Hai Nan Sheng, San Ya Shi, Tian Ya Qu, 225国道天涯镇马岭山麓 邮政编码: 572029
tianya-haijiao.description: This ticket office serves as the gateway to Tianya-haijiao, where massive ancient boulders inscribed with Chinese calligraphy mark the legendary 'ends of the earth' along Sanya's southern coastline. Visit to access one of Hainan's most romantic landmarks, where couples flock to witness the famous love-themed rock formations by the turquoise sea.
tianya-haijiao.tip: Purchase tickets online before arriving to skip queues, especially during Chinese holidays. The scenic area is large, so wear comfortable walking shoes and bring sun protection.

yalong-bay.name: 呀诺达雨林
yalong-bay.address: Baoting Li and Miao Autonomous County, China, 572316
yalong-bay.description: This expansive tropical rainforest park near Yalong Bay features elevated walkways winding through dense jungle, waterfalls, and a thrilling valley zipline experience. The lush biodiversity and interactive eco-adventures make it Sanya's premier rainforest escape.
yalong-bay.tip: Arrive right at opening to avoid tour groups and experience the rainforest in peaceful morning mist. Wear sturdy shoes as pathways can be slippery after rain.

yalong-bay.name: Yalongwan
yalong-bay.address: Yalongwan, Jiyang District, Sanya, Sanya, China, 572011
yalong-bay.description: This crescent-shaped bay boasts powdery white sand and crystal-clear turquoise waters framed by tropical palms and lush green hills. The pristine 7-kilometer beach offers some of Hainan's calmest swimming conditions and most vibrant coral reefs just offshore.
yalong-bay.tip: Arrive before 9am to claim prime beach spots near the public access points before tour groups arrive, and bring snorkeling gear to explore the shallow coral gardens on the bay's eastern end.

yalong-bay.name: 槟榔谷
yalong-bay.address: 9MX6+3H2, 224 Guo Dao, Bao Ting Li Zu Miao Zu Zi Zhi Xian, Hai Nan Sheng, China, 572317
yalong-bay.summary: Large cultural park & village showcasing local customs, cuisine & arts with live performances.
yalong-bay.description: This expansive cultural village immerses visitors in the traditions of Hainan's Li and Miao ethnic minorities through authentic architecture, intricate textile demonstrations, and daily folk performances. The live tattoo ceremonies and traditional wedding shows offer rare glimpses into indigenous customs rarely seen elsewhere in modern China.
yalong-bay.tip: Arrive by 10am to watch elderly Li women demonstrate traditional face tattooing techniques and brocade weaving before tour groups crowd the workshops after lunch.

yalong-bay.name: Yanuoda Rainforest Tourism Area
yalong-bay.chinese: 呀诺达雨林文化旅游区
yalong-bay.address: FJ4X+6PG, Baoting Li and Miao Autonomous County, China, 572316
yalong-bay.description: This expansive tropical rainforest park features canopy walkways suspended high among ancient trees, cascading waterfalls, and well-marked trails through diverse rainforest ecosystems. The combination of accessible hiking paths and thrilling zip-line courses makes it perfect for experiencing Hainan's jungle terrain without venturing off-grid.
yalong-bay.tip: Arrive right at opening to hike the trails before tour groups. The Valley of Dreams trail is less crowded than the main route and offers better wildlife spotting opportunities.

gulangyu-island.name: Haoyueyuan
gulangyu-island.address: 3 Zhang Zhou Lu, Si Ming Qu, Xia Men Shi, Fu Jian Sheng, China, 361002
gulangyu-island.description: This seaside garden on Gulangyu Island features a striking 15-meter statue of Koxinga gazing across the strait toward Taiwan. The clifftop location offers expansive views of Xiamen's skyline and provides a peaceful escape from the island's crowded pedestrian streets.
gulangyu-island.tip: Visit during late afternoon when tour groups have left and the setting sun illuminates the statue. The rocks along the shoreline make excellent viewing spots.

gulangyu-island.name: Longshandong
gulangyu-island.address: C3X9+4PM, Gu Xin Lu, Si Ming Qu, Xia Men Shi, Fu Jian Sheng, China, 361001
gulangyu-island.description: This natural cave formation on Gulangyu Island features dramatic rock formations and cool chambers that provided shelter to locals throughout history. The intimate scale and geological formations offer a peaceful escape from the island's busier attractions.
gulangyu-island.tip: Visit during midday when most tourists are having lunch—the cave stays naturally cool and you'll have the space mostly to yourself.

gulangyu-island.name: Gulangyu Organ Museum
gulangyu-island.address: 43 Gu Xin Lu, Si Ming Qu, Xia Men Shi, Fu Jian Sheng, China, 361210
gulangyu-island.description: China's only organ museum houses over 70 antique pipe organs and reed organs from Europe and America, some dating back to the 1800s, displayed inside the grand Bagua Building. Daily concerts let you hear these rare instruments come alive with classical melodies echoing through ornate colonial halls.
gulangyu-island.tip: Visit at 10:30am or 3:30pm for the free organ concerts when staff demonstrate the historic instruments. The second floor has the most impressive pieces with detailed mechanical displays.

gulangyu-island.name: 日光岩景区
gulangyu-island.address: 62 Huang Yan Lu, Si Ming Qu, Xia Men Shi, Fu Jian Sheng, China, 361001
gulangyu-island.summary: Hilltop woodland with boulders, pavilions & terraces, plus views of the coast & Xiamen at sunrise.
gulangyu-island.description: This hilltop sanctuary on Gulangyu Island features massive natural boulders, traditional pavilions, and winding stone paths through shaded woodland leading to Xiamen's highest coastal viewpoint. Arrive at dawn to watch the sunrise paint the harbor and cityscape in golden light while standing 92 meters above sea level.
gulangyu-island.tip: Take the right-hand path up for fewer crowds and better photo angles of the rock formations. The left path is wider but gets congested with tour groups mid-morning.

gulangyu-island.name: Gulangyu Piano Museum
gulangyu-island.address: 45 Huang Yan Lu, Si Ming Qu, Xia Men Shi, Fu Jian Sheng, China, 361001
gulangyu-island.summary: Vintage Steinways, gold-plated & self-playing instruments, displayed in a tranquil seaside pavilion.
gulangyu-island.description: This intimate museum houses over 100 rare pianos including gold-plated European instruments and antique self-playing models collected by overseas Chinese pianist Hu Youyi. The oceanfront setting inside Shuzhuang Garden creates a peaceful atmosphere where hourly automated concerts let you hear these century-old pianos still perform.
gulangyu-island.tip: Visit during the top of odd hours (9am, 11am, 1pm, 3pm) when staff demonstrate the self-playing pianos—the automated performances showcase the instruments far better than viewing them silent.

gulangyu-island.name: Qinyuan （North Gate）
gulangyu-island.address: C3R8+V22, An Hai Lu, Si Ming Qu, Xia Men Shi, Fu Jian Sheng, China, 361002
gulangyu-island.description: This elegant seaside garden on Gulangyu Island features traditional Minnan architecture with pavilions perched over tide pools and a famous piano museum housed within. The northern entrance offers direct access to the musical instrument collection and coastal boardwalks with views across to mainland Xiamen.
gulangyu-island.tip: Enter through the north gate early morning to avoid cruise ship tour groups that typically arrive via the main entrance after 10am.

gulangyu-island.name: Hi Heaven
gulangyu-island.address: 38 Fu Jian Lu, Si Ming Qu, Xia Men Shi, Fu Jian Sheng, China, 361001
gulangyu-island.description: Perched at the highest point of Gulangyu Island, this massive granite rock formation offers panoramic views across Xiamen's harbor and cityscape through winding staircases carved into the stone. The 360-degree vista from the summit, where you can watch ferries glide between the island and mainland, makes the climb worthwhile.
gulangyu-island.tip: Arrive right when it opens at 8am to avoid tour groups and enjoy clearer morning light for photos across the strait to mainland Xiamen.

gulangyu-island.name: Gulangyu
gulangyu-island.description: This car-free island off Xiamen's coast features winding lanes lined with colonial-era mansions, hidden courtyards, and over 200 historic pianos that earned it the nickname 'Piano Island'. The blend of traditional southern Fujian architecture with European buildings creates a uniquely photogenic waterfront escape.
gulangyu-island.tip: Take the ferry from Dongdu Cruise Terminal instead of the main tourist pier to arrive at less crowded spots on the island's quieter east side.

gulangyu-island.name: Yuyuan Garden
gulangyu-island.address: China, Fu Jian Sheng, Xia Men Shi, Si Ming Qu, Fu Xing Lu, 鼓浪屿 邮政编码: 361001
gulangyu-island.description: This intimate classical Chinese garden in Gulangyu offers winding pathways through rockeries, koi ponds, and pavilions draped with bougainvillea and bamboo. The peaceful refuge provides an authentic taste of traditional garden design just steps from the island's busy tourist lanes.
gulangyu-island.tip: Visit early morning around 7-8am when local residents practice tai chi near the entrance pond, and the soft light filters beautifully through the garden's mature trees.

gulangyu-island.name: 鼓浪环岛路
gulangyu-island.address: F327+HW2, Siming District, Xiamen, Fujian, China, 361002
gulangyu-island.description: This coastal walking path circles Gulangyu Island, offering glimpses of colonial architecture, rocky shorelines, and quiet beaches away from the main tourist crowds. The best reason to visit is experiencing the island's natural coastline while discovering secluded spots that most day-trippers miss.
gulangyu-island.tip: Start your walk early from Neitian Beach heading clockwise to catch morning light on the east coast and avoid tour groups who rarely venture beyond the main attractions.

gulangyu-island.name: Shuzhuang Zanghai
gulangyu-island.address: C3QC+P3W, Tian Wei Lu, Si Ming Qu, Xia Men Shi, Fu Jian Sheng, China, 361001
gulangyu-island.description: This clifftop garden on Gulangyu Island cleverly uses traditional Chinese landscaping to hide and reveal ocean views through moon gates and winding pathways. The Piano Museum housed within displays over 100 antique pianos from around the world, offering a unique blend of nature and musical heritage.
gulangyu-island.tip: Visit the Twelve Cave Heaven rockery area at high tide when waves crash through the hollowed rocks, creating dramatic natural fountains that spray seawater into the garden.

gulangyu-island.name: Xiamen Kulangsu Scenery Spot-Guanhaiyuan Sandy Beach
gulangyu-island.chinese: 厦门鼓浪屿风景名胜区-观海园沙滩
gulangyu-island.address: Xiamen Kulangsu Scenery Spot-Guanhaiyuan Sandy Beach, Siming District, Xiamen, China, 361001
gulangyu-island.description: This sheltered sandy beach on Gulangyu Island offers calm, shallow waters ideal for wading and a peaceful escape from the island's busier tourist spots. The combination of soft sand, clear water, and views of colonial-era villas perched on surrounding hills makes it perfect for relaxed seaside hours.
gulangyu-island.tip: Visit during weekday mornings when day-trippers haven't yet arrived from the mainland ferry terminal, giving you nearly private beach access.

gulangyu-island.name: 菽庄花园景区
gulangyu-island.address: China, Fu Jian Sheng, Xia Men Shi, Si Ming Qu, 鼓浪屿 邮政编码: 361001
gulangyu-island.description: This early 20th-century garden masterfully blends classical Chinese landscaping with coastal views, featuring moon gates, rockeries, and the unique Piano Museum housed within. The intimate scale and seaside setting create a tranquil contrast to Gulangyu's busier colonial streets.
gulangyu-island.tip: Visit the Piano Museum upstairs during scheduled demonstrations to hear rare antique instruments being played. The garden's western edge offers the best photo angles of rock formations framing the ocean.

gulangyu-island.name: Shuzhuang Garden
gulangyu-island.description: Built in 1913 by a Taiwanese merchant, this classical Chinese garden cleverly uses borrowed scenery techniques to frame ocean views between rockeries and moon gates. The piano museum housed within its pavilions displays over 100 antique pianos collected from around the world.
gulangyu-island.tip: Visit the Forty-Four Bridge during mid-morning when fewer tourists are around—the structure's reflection in the pond creates perfect symmetry for photos without crowds blocking the view.

gulangyu-island.name: Zheng Chenggong Memorial Hall
gulangyu-island.address: 73 Yong Chun Lu, Si Ming Qu, Xia Men Shi, Fu Jian Sheng, China, 361001
gulangyu-island.description: This memorial hall honors Zheng Chenggong, the Ming dynasty loyalist who expelled Dutch colonizers from Taiwan in 1662, with exhibits of historical artifacts, military equipment, and personal belongings displayed across traditional architecture. The highlight is the detailed dioramas recreating naval battles that showcase his strategic brilliance and legacy as a national hero.
gulangyu-island.tip: Visit during weekday mornings when local school groups aren't present for quieter viewing. The second floor has English explanations that provide better context than ground floor displays.

gulangyu-island.name: Xiamen Tourism Passenger Wharf
gulangyu-island.address: 33 Lu Jiang Dao, Si Ming Qu, Xia Men Shi, Fu Jian Sheng, China, 361001
gulangyu-island.description: This bustling ferry terminal serves as the main gateway to Gulangyu Island, with frequent departures and a constant flow of travelers eager to explore the car-free island across the water. The wharf offers the most convenient and affordable way to reach one of Xiamen's most beloved destinations.
gulangyu-island.tip: Take the early morning ferries before 9am to avoid massive crowds, especially on weekends and holidays. Purchase round-trip tickets through WeChat to skip the ticket counter lines.

siming-south.name: Wuyuanwan Wetland Park
siming-south.address: Huli District, Xiamen, China, 361016
siming-south.description: This expansive urban wetland sanctuary features wooden boardwalks winding through reed beds and lagoons, attracting migratory birds including black swans and egrets year-round. The peaceful waterside trails offer a rare natural escape within Xiamen's modern Siming district, perfect for easy cycling or leisurely walks.
siming-south.tip: Visit the eastern section near the lake island early morning for the best bird photography opportunities when species are most active and lighting is ideal.

siming-south.name: Gulangyu-Wanshi Mountain National Key Scenic Area
siming-south.chinese: 鼓浪屿-万石山国家重点风景名胜区
siming-south.address: F33X+42V, Siming District, Xiamen, Fujian, China, 361003
siming-south.description: This expansive scenic area combines the car-free colonial island of Gulangyu with the tropical botanical gardens and hiking trails of Wanshi Mountain on the mainland. The dual landscapes offer both maritime heritage architecture and lush mountain greenery within a single protected zone.
siming-south.tip: Access Gulangyu section via ferry from Xiamen International Cruise Center terminal rather than tourist piers for cheaper fares and shorter queues, especially on weekends.

siming-south.name: Nanhu Park （South Gate）
siming-south.address: 12 Hu Guang Lu, Si Ming Qu, Xia Men Shi, Fu Jian Sheng, China, 361000
siming-south.description: This beloved local park wraps around a serene lake, with tree-lined pathways, traditional pavilions, and locals practicing tai chi at dawn. The waterfront promenade offers peaceful views and a genuine glimpse into everyday Xiamen life away from tourist crowds.
siming-south.tip: Enter through the South Gate early morning to join locals doing group exercises and dancing. The lakeside tea houses offer affordable refreshments with the best water views.

siming-south.name: 白鹭洲公园
siming-south.address: F3FR+7V6, Bai Lu Zhou Dong Lu, Si Ming Qu, Xia Men Shi, Fu Jian Sheng, China, 361000
siming-south.description: This urban park sits on an island in Yundang Lake, featuring a musical fountain, landscaped gardens, and waterfront promenades with views of Xiamen's modern skyline. The evening musical fountain show set against illuminated city towers offers a relaxing escape in the heart of Siming district.
siming-south.tip: Visit on weekend evenings around 8pm for the musical fountain show, but arrive early to secure a good viewing spot along the lakeside railings.

siming-south.name: 金榜公园西门
siming-south.address: F475+JJ8, Siming District, Xiamen, Fujian, China, 361010
siming-south.description: This western entrance to Jinbang Park offers easy access to tree-lined paths and hilltop views over Xiamen's Siming District. The park provides a peaceful escape with traditional pavilions and shaded walking trails popular among local residents.
siming-south.tip: Enter through this gate in early morning to join locals doing tai chi and traditional exercises, then follow the uphill path for the best city vantage points.

siming-south.name: Xiamen Science and Technology Museum
siming-south.address: China, Fu Jian Sheng, Xia Men Shi, Si Ming Qu, 体育路95号F4R5+476 邮政编码: 361012
siming-south.description: This interactive science museum features hands-on exhibits covering robotics, aerospace, earthquake simulators, and a 4D cinema that engages visitors of all ages. The earthquake experience hall lets you feel simulated tremors while learning about disaster preparedness in an unforgettable way.
siming-south.tip: Visit on weekday mornings to avoid school groups and weekend crowds. The 4D cinema shows run on schedule, so check times immediately upon arrival and book your preferred slot first.

siming-south.name: South Putuo Temple
siming-south.address: 515 Si Ming Nan Lu, Si Ming Qu, Xia Men Shi, Fu Jian Sheng, China, 361005
siming-south.summary: Tang Dynasty–era Buddhist temple in spacious landscaped grounds featuring terraces, pools & pagodas.
siming-south.description: This Tang Dynasty Buddhist temple features five grand halls ascending the hillside, with devout monks chanting daily and ornate pavilions overlooking Xiamen University and the sea. The vegetarian restaurant serves exceptional Buddhist cuisine that draws locals and pilgrims year-round.
siming-south.tip: Arrive by 6:30am to witness morning chanting ceremonies with the monks, an atmospheric experience few tourists see. Exit through the back gate to hike Wulao Peak for temple views.

siming-south.name: Xiamen Calligraphy Square
siming-south.address: C4H9+4R3, Huan Dao Nan Lu, Si Ming Qu, Xia Men Shi, Fu Jian Sheng, China, 361005
siming-south.description: This open-air plaza showcases monumental stone installations featuring classical Chinese calligraphy, providing an artistic gateway to understanding traditional ink art forms. The peaceful setting offers a rare chance to photograph large-scale calligraphic works alongside Xiamen's urban landscape.
siming-south.tip: Visit during early morning when local practitioners gather to practice water calligraphy on the ground—watch their brushwork and some may offer informal demonstrations of technique.

siming-south.name: Zengcuo'an Wenchuang Fishing Vlg.
siming-south.address: China, Xia Men Shi, Si Ming Qu, 正南方向20米 邮政编码: 361005
siming-south.description: This former fishing village has transformed into a bohemian enclave filled with indie cafes, street art murals, and artisan shops tucked into narrow alleyways beside the sea. The village offers an authentic glimpse into local Minnan architecture while serving up some of Xiamen's best seafood snacks and creative boutiques.
siming-south.tip: Visit the small temple on the eastern side early morning to see elderly residents practicing their daily rituals, then grab fresh oyster omelettes from the stalls near the beach entrance.

siming-south.name: 万石山风景区
siming-south.address: China, Fu Jian Sheng, Xia Men Shi, Si Ming Qu, 不见天 邮政编码: 361005
siming-south.description: This expansive hillside park in Siming features winding paths through tropical botanical gardens, ancient rock formations, and temples tucked among dense greenery. The combination of accessible hiking trails and diverse plant species makes it a refreshing urban escape with surprising biodiversity.
siming-south.tip: Visit the Wanshi Botanical Garden section early morning when locals practice tai chi near the rainforest valley—the misty atmosphere creates perfect conditions for photography and the crowds haven't arrived yet.

siming-south.name: Xiamen Taiwan Folk Village
siming-south.address: China, Fu Jian Sheng, Xia Men Shi, Si Ming Qu, Huan Dao Nan Lu, 环岛路35号 邮政编码: 361005
siming-south.description: This cultural theme park recreates traditional Taiwanese village life with authentic architecture, folk performances, and handicraft demonstrations. The immersive experience offers a glimpse into Taiwan's rural heritage without leaving mainland China, featuring traditional courtyard houses and live cultural shows.
siming-south.tip: Visit during scheduled folk performance times (usually mid-morning and early afternoon) to catch traditional puppet shows and aboriginal dance demonstrations that bring the village to life.

siming-south.name: Lu Xun Memorial Hall
siming-south.address: China, Fu Jian Sheng, Xia Men Shi, Si Ming Qu, Si Ming Nan Lu, 422号厦门大学集美楼2楼 邮政编码: 361005
siming-south.description: This memorial hall chronicles the life and revolutionary writings of Lu Xun, modern China's most influential writer who lived in Xiamen in 1926. The carefully curated exhibits showcase original manuscripts, personal belongings, and photographs that illuminate his impact on Chinese literature and social reform.
siming-south.tip: Visit the small garden courtyard behind the main hall where Lu Xun used to take evening walks—it's rarely crowded and offers a contemplative space away from the exhibition rooms.

siming-south.name: Overseas Chinese Museum
siming-south.address: 493 Si Ming Nan Lu, Si Ming Qu, Xia Men Shi, Fu Jian Sheng, China, 361005
siming-south.summary: Museum detailing the life of Chinese people living overseas, with historical & cultural artifacts.
siming-south.description: This comprehensive museum chronicles the journeys and contributions of Chinese diaspora communities worldwide through historic photographs, documents, and artifacts spanning centuries of migration. The fascinating collection reveals untold stories of overseas Chinese entrepreneurship, cultural preservation, and connections between Xiamen and the wider Chinese diaspora across Southeast Asia and beyond.
siming-south.tip: Visit the second floor exhibition halls first, which showcase the most compelling personal stories and artifacts from Southeast Asian Chinese communities, including rare documents from early merchant families.

siming-south.name: Zhongshan Park （South Gate）
siming-south.address: F35Q+5MW, Gong Yuan Dong Lu, Si Ming Qu, Xia Men Shi, Fu Jian Sheng, China, 361001
siming-south.description: This historic urban park features centuries-old banyan trees, traditional pavilions, and serene lotus ponds that offer a peaceful escape in the heart of Xiamen. The South Gate entrance provides direct access to the park's renowned bonsai garden and morning tai chi gatherings where locals practice alongside the lake.
siming-south.tip: Enter through the South Gate early morning to join free tai chi sessions with friendly locals who often welcome newcomers to learn basic moves by the lakeside.

siming-south.name: Hongshan Park （West Gate 1）
siming-south.address: C3WP+69F, Si Ming Nan Lu, Si Ming Qu, Xia Men Shi, Fu Jian Sheng, China, 361001
siming-south.description: This hillside park in Siming offers winding paths through lush greenery and open-air exercise areas where locals practice tai chi at dawn. The elevated trails provide peaceful views over Xiamen's residential neighborhoods without the tourist crowds found at more famous spots.
siming-south.tip: Enter through the west gate early morning to join free group exercise sessions led by local retirees—they're welcoming to visitors and it's a genuine cultural experience.

siming-south.name: Yuanlin Botanical Garden
siming-south.address: Siming District, Xiamen, China
siming-south.description: This expansive botanical garden showcases themed plant collections across valleys and hillsides, including a renowned succulent greenhouse and a bamboo forest that feels worlds away from urban Xiamen. The tropical rainforest section recreates Southeast Asian jungle environments with towering palms and exotic orchids that will feel familiar yet curated to regional visitors.
siming-south.tip: Enter through the west gate near Wanshi Reservoir for easier uphill walks and better light for photos in the morning. The electric cart service saves energy for exploring upper sections.

siming-south.name: Xianyue Park
siming-south.address: F4X6+PP8, Qiao Yue Lu, Si Ming Qu, Xia Men Shi, Fu Jian Sheng, China, 361006
siming-south.description: This hillside park features winding trails through dense bamboo groves and tropical vegetation, with panoramic views over Xiamen's urban skyline and Yundang Lake. The peaceful mountain atmosphere offers a refreshing escape from the city heat, making it a favorite morning exercise spot for locals practicing tai chi and traditional dance.
siming-south.tip: Take the eastern trail entrance near Lianqian West Road for a gentler ascent and stop at the small temple midway up for the best photo angles of the city below.

siming-south.name: Roundabout Binhai Trails
siming-south.address: China, Fu Jian Sheng, Xia Men Shi, Si Ming Qu, Hu Li Shan, 炮台附近 邮政编码: 361005
siming-south.description: This coastal walking trail winds along Xiamen's southeastern shore, offering uninterrupted ocean views, rocky outcrops, and a refreshing sea breeze away from the city's bustle. The perfect spot for a leisurely seaside stroll where you can watch local fishermen at work and cyclists passing by on the adjacent bike path.
siming-south.tip: Visit during late afternoon to catch golden hour light on the water, and look for the small pavilions along the route where locals gather to play cards and chat.

siming-south.name: Huihe Stone Cultural Park
siming-south.address: F5Q2+392, Lv Ling Lu, Hu Li Qu, Xia Men Shi, Fu Jian Sheng, China, 361009
siming-south.description: This unique park showcases intricately carved stone sculptures and traditional Chinese stone craft artistry set within peaceful gardens. The perfect spot for those seeking a quiet cultural escape that reveals Xiamen's lesser-known artistic heritage.
siming-south.tip: Visit the workshop area on weekday mornings to watch local artisans hand-carving stone pieces using traditional techniques passed down through generations.

siming-south.name: Yundang College
siming-south.address: F3HX+533, Hu Bin Zhong Lu, Si Ming Qu, Xia Men Shi, Fu Jian Sheng, China, 361000
siming-south.description: This historic academy on the shores of Yundang Lake blends traditional Fujian architecture with serene waterfront gardens, serving as a cultural center for calligraphy and classical arts. Visit to experience traditional Chinese scholarly atmosphere while enjoying peaceful lake views away from Xiamen's busier tourist sites.
siming-south.tip: Check the schedule for free calligraphy demonstrations and tea ceremonies held on weekend afternoons, when local masters showcase traditional Fujian artistic techniques.

siming-south.name: Huweishan Park （South Gate）
siming-south.address: F3HH+6WQ, Hu Bin Bei Lu, Si Ming Qu, Xia Men Shi, Fu Jian Sheng, China, 361012
siming-south.description: This hillside park in Siming South offers winding trails through lush greenery and terraced gardens, with quiet pavilions overlooking residential Xiamen. The elevated vantage points provide intimate views of the city's daily rhythm without the crowds of larger tourist parks.
siming-south.tip: Enter through the south gate in the early morning to join locals practicing tai chi and traditional exercises under the banyan trees—a authentic slice of neighborhood life.

siming-south.name: 湿地公园
siming-south.address: G59G+37W, Huli District, Xiamen, Fujian, China, 361016
siming-south.description: This expansive wetland park in Siming District features wooden boardwalks winding through marshes, mangrove forests, and waterbird habitats. Visit during early morning hours to spot migrating birds and watch locals practicing tai chi beside tranquil ponds.
siming-south.tip: Bring mosquito repellent, especially in warmer months. The eastern entrance near Yuandang Lake offers the best bird-watching spots with fewer crowds than the main entrance.

siming-south.name: Strange Slope
siming-south.address: China, Xia Men Shi, Si Ming Qu, CN 福建省 厦门市 思明区 30 米 邮政编码: 361010
siming-south.description: This gravity-defying optical illusion creates the sensation that objects roll uphill against gravity on a sloped road section. Experience the bizarre phenomenon firsthand by watching water appear to flow upward and vehicles seemingly coast against natural laws.
siming-south.tip: Visit early morning on weekdays to avoid tourist crowds and get better photos. Bring a bottle of water to test the uphill flow effect yourself for the most memorable experience.

siming-south.name: Shangli Reservoir Park
siming-south.address: 866-868 Long Hu Shan Lu, 866, Si Ming Qu, Xia Men Shi, Fu Jian Sheng, China, 361010
siming-south.description: This peaceful reservoir park in Siming's southern hills offers tree-lined walking paths along calm waters, popular with locals for morning exercises and quiet reflection. The serene lakeside setting provides a refreshing escape from Xiamen's urban bustle without traveling far from the city center.
siming-south.tip: Visit early morning to join local tai chi groups by the water's edge and experience the park at its most tranquil before afternoon crowds arrive.

jimei.name: 鳌园
jimei.address: China, Fu Jian Sheng, Xia Men Shi, Ji Mei Qu, 嘉庚路24号 邮政编码: 361021
jimei.description: This memorial garden honors Tan Kah Kee, Southeast Asia's legendary philanthropist, with elaborate stone carvings depicting Chinese history and mythology along its seaside grounds. The fusion of Southern Fujian architecture and intricate marble reliefs creates an outdoor museum celebrating the overseas Chinese diaspora.
jimei.tip: Visit the tomb pavilion at the garden's center to see the detailed biographical carvings that trace Tan Kah Kee's journey from Singapore rubber magnate to education pioneer.

jimei.name: Old Courtyard Style Garden Scenic
jimei.address: 19 Hua Xia Lu, Ji Mei Qu, Xia Men Shi, Fu Jian Sheng, China, 361024
jimei.description: This traditional Minnan-style courtyard complex in Jimei recreates historic Xiamen village life with restored buildings, folk performance stages, and cultural exhibits showcasing southern Fujian heritage. The immersive architecture and detailed period recreations offer visitors an authentic glimpse into the region's courtyard culture and customs.
jimei.tip: Visit during weekday mornings to avoid tour groups and enjoy the peaceful courtyard atmosphere. Look for the traditional puppet show performances held in the main hall.

jimei.name: Xiangshan Park
jimei.description: This hillside park in Jimei offers peaceful walking trails through lush vegetation and panoramic views of Xiamen's coastline and Jimei School Village below. The elevated vantage points provide excellent photo opportunities of the surrounding islands and traditional architecture without the crowds of more central attractions.
jimei.tip: Visit early morning when local residents practice tai chi near the entrance pavilion, and take the less-traveled eastern trail for the best views of Jimei's Tan Kah Kee architecture.

jimei.name: Sanglian Historic Site
jimei.address: WH7P+M3C, Xin Hua Bei Lu, Li Cheng Qu, Quan Zhou Shi, Fu Jian Sheng, China, 362001
jimei.description: This historic site in Jimei preserves ancient architectural remains and cultural relics that showcase the area's maritime heritage and early settlement patterns. The well-preserved structures offer an authentic glimpse into traditional Minnan culture away from commercial tourist crowds.
jimei.tip: Visit during weekday mornings when local volunteers sometimes offer impromptu historical explanations in Hokkien dialect, providing deeper context than any signage could convey.

jimei.name: Xiamen Tianzhu Mountain Tourism Scenic Spot
jimei.address: HW6Q+C5X, Fu Lian Lu, Hai Cang Qu, Xia Men Shi, Fu Jian Sheng, China, 361027
jimei.description: This forested mountain park in Jimei offers hiking trails through bamboo groves and pine forests, leading to temples and pavilions with views over the district. The peaceful escape from urban Xiamen combines light exercise with glimpses of local religious life at scattered Buddhist shrines.
jimei.tip: Start early morning to avoid heat and catch locals practicing tai chi near the main temple. Bring water as vendors are sparse beyond the entrance area.

jimei.name: Lu Gangzhang Park
jimei.address: China, Fu Jian Sheng, Xia Men Shi, Tong An Qu, 422县道公园道1号 邮政编码: 361110
jimei.description: This peaceful neighborhood park in Jimei honors local educator Lu Gangzhang with traditional landscaping, shaded walking paths, and exercise equipment popular with residents. The quiet atmosphere and authentic glimpse into daily life away from Xiamen's tourist crowds makes it worth a short visit.
jimei.tip: Visit in early morning to see locals practicing tai chi and traditional exercises, offering great opportunities for cultural observation and friendly interactions with Jimei residents.

jimei.name: The Jimei School Village
jimei.address: 25 Shi Gu Lu, Ji Mei Qu, Xia Men Shi, Fu Jian Sheng, China, 361021
jimei.description: This distinctive early 20th-century educational complex showcases Tan Kah Kee's architectural vision, blending traditional Chinese roofs with Western colonial structures across sprawling campuses and gardens. The Turtle Garden Pond and ornate heritage buildings offer a peaceful escape into Xiamen's scholarly past.
jimei.tip: Visit the Dragon Boat Pond area early morning when local students practice tai chi. The main gate area is best photographed in late afternoon when the light hits the red-tiled roofs.

jimei.name: Mr. Chen Jiageng Former Residence
jimei.address: 149 Jia Geng Lu, Ji Mei Qu, Xia Men Shi, Fu Jian Sheng, China, 361021
jimei.description: This traditional Fujian-style mansion showcases the private life of overseas Chinese philanthropist Chen Jiageng, who funded hundreds of schools across Southeast Asia and China in the early 1900s. The preserved rooms display his personal belongings, correspondence with regional leaders, and photographs documenting his legacy in Singapore, Malaysia, and beyond.
jimei.tip: Visit the adjacent Tan Kah Kee Memorial Hall first to understand his broader impact before seeing his actual living quarters—it provides essential context that makes the residence far more meaningful.

jimei.name: 集美学村
jimei.address: China, Fu Jian Sheng, Xia Men Shi, Ji Mei Qu, 厦门大桥下集美学村 邮政编码: 361021
jimei.description: This historic educational complex blends traditional Chinese architecture with Western colonial influences, featuring red-tiled roofs, ornate courtyards, and waterfront pavilions built by philanthropist Tan Kah Kee. The Dragon Boat Pond offers peaceful walks along tree-lined paths where students and locals gather, creating an authentic glimpse into Xiamen's scholarly heritage.
jimei.tip: Visit the Jiageng Park area early morning to see elderly residents practicing tai chi and traditional music. The cafes near Longzhou Pond serve excellent local breakfast at student-friendly prices.

jimei.name: Xiamen Tianzhushan Forest Park
jimei.address: HWJ8+M6F, Haicang District, Xiamen, Fujian, China, 361027
jimei.description: This forest park in Jimei district features lush hiking trails winding through dense subtropical vegetation and ancient rock formations dotting the mountainside. The peaceful escape from urban Xiamen offers fresh air and panoramic views over the district without the crowds of more famous peaks.
jimei.tip: Start early morning to enjoy cooler temperatures and clearer views before afternoon haze sets in. Local hikers recommend the trail leading to the summit's temple ruins.

tulou-day-trip.name: Chuxi Tulou cluster
tulou-day-trip.address: China, Fu Jian Sheng, Long Yan Shi, Yong Ding Qu, 洋湖线GWX2+HR6 邮政编码: 364113
tulou-day-trip.summary: Hakka cultural site featuring unique, communal homes dating back to the 15th century.
tulou-day-trip.description: These massive circular earthen fortresses rise from the countryside like ancient UFOs, with thick rammed-earth walls housing entire Hakka clans in wooden galleries arranged around central courtyards. Walk through living heritage where families still cook, work, and gather as their ancestors did 600 years ago.
tulou-day-trip.tip: Visit Jiqing Lou, the oldest structure here from 1419. Climb to the third floor for symmetrical courtyard photos and chat with elderly residents who sell tea and homemade snacks.

tulou-day-trip.name: Chengqilou
tulou-day-trip.summary: Large, circular, 18th-c. earthen building with hundreds of individual dwellings & communal kitchens.
tulou-day-trip.description: This 300-year-old circular tulou houses four concentric rings and rises four stories high, with nearly 400 rooms that once sheltered 80 families in a remarkable communal living system. Walk through the inner courtyards to see how Hakka clans designed earthquake-resistant earthen architecture that maintained perfect symmetry.
tulou-day-trip.tip: Visit the third-floor corridor for the best perspective of the concentric circles. Local residents still live here, so respect privacy and ask before photographing people.

huangshan-front.name: Huangshan Scenic Area
huangshan-front.address: China, An Hui Sheng, Huang Shan Shi, Huang Shan Qu, 汤口镇44JH+XX7 邮政编码: 245700
huangshan-front.description: Ancient granite peaks rise through swirling seas of clouds, their twisted pine trees clinging to sheer cliff faces like natural bonsai sculptures. The otherworldly landscape has inspired Chinese painters and poets for over a thousand years, creating vistas that feel like stepping into a classical scroll painting.
huangshan-front.tip: Start your ascent before dawn to reach the peaks for sunrise, when the cloud sea phenomenon is most dramatic and tourist crowds are thinnest.

huangshan-front.name: Huangshan Mountain
huangshan-front.address: 45V6+V27, Huangshan District, Huangshan City, Anhui, China, 245801
huangshan-front.description: Ancient granite peaks emerge from rolling seas of clouds, their weather-sculpted forms dotted with twisted pines clinging to sheer rock faces. The otherworldly landscape has inspired Chinese painters and poets for over a thousand years, offering hiking trails that wind past hot springs and through mist-shrouded valleys.
huangshan-front.tip: Start your ascent before dawn from the eastern steps to catch sunrise at Lion Peak, then take the cable car down to save your knees for the next day.

huangshan-front.name: Huangshan Tourism
huangshan-front.description: The legendary granite peaks rise through swirling seas of clouds, their ancient pine trees clinging to impossibly steep cliffsides like nature's own calligraphy. Watching sunrise paint the rock formations in shades of gold while mist dances through valleys below is an experience that has inspired Chinese artists for centuries.
huangshan-front.tip: Start your ascent by 4am to reach the summit before sunrise crowds arrive. The western steps are less crowded than the eastern route but equally rewarding.

huangshan-front.name: 天都峰
huangshan-front.address: China, An Hui Sheng, Huang Shan Shi, Huang Shan Qu, 黄山风景区内 邮政编码: 245899
huangshan-front.description: This jagged granite peak rises 1,810 meters and features nearly-vertical stone staircases carved into cliffsides, including the famous "Carp's Backbone" ridgeline section with 1,600 steps. The summit rewards climbers with sweeping views across Huangshan's sea of peaks and pine forests that few other vantage points can match.
huangshan-front.tip: Start your climb by 7am to avoid crowds and afternoon fog that often obscures views. The peak closes periodically for maintenance during winter months, so check current status before planning your visit.

huangshan-front.name: Huangshan Mountain Yesheng Hougu Scenic Area
huangshan-front.address: 35C4+HVQ, Huangshan District, Huangshan City, Anhui, China, 245800
huangshan-front.description: This scenic valley area near Huangshan Mountain features wild monkey habitats where visitors can observe troops of macaques in their natural forest environment. The chance to safely interact with and photograph semi-wild monkeys against the backdrop of mountain scenery is the main draw for families and wildlife enthusiasts.
huangshan-front.tip: Visit in early morning when monkeys are most active and hungry, making them more likely to approach viewing areas. Bring peanuts sold at entrance for feeding.

huangshan-front.name: 黄山猴子观海
huangshan-front.address: China, An Hui Sheng, Huang Shan Shi, Huang Shan Qu, 黄山风景区 邮政编码: 245700
huangshan-front.description: This famous rock formation resembles a monkey crouching at the edge of a cliff, gazing out over the sea of clouds that frequently blankets Huangshan's valleys. Visit during misty mornings to witness the monkey seemingly float above an ethereal white ocean below.
huangshan-front.tip: Arrive before 8am when clouds are thickest and crowds thinnest. The viewing platform gets congested by mid-morning, especially during holidays.

huangshan-front.name: Huangshan Hot Spring
huangshan-front.address: 452F+4P6, Huangshan District, Huangshan City, Anhui, China, 245899
huangshan-front.description: Natural hot springs emerge at 42°C from beneath the peaks of Huangshan, rich in minerals believed to soothe tired muscles after mountain hikes. The therapeutic waters offer the perfect remedy for weary legs following a day exploring the famous granite peaks above.
huangshan-front.tip: Visit in the early morning when the springs are less crowded and mist rises dramatically from the heated pools against the cool mountain air.

huangshan-front.name: Huangshan Scenic Area Cultural Center
huangshan-front.address: 35MR+HC3, Tang Quan Lu, Huang Shan Qu, Huang Shan Shi, An Hui Sheng, China, 245899
huangshan-front.description: This modern cultural center serves as the gateway orientation hub for Huangshan Mountain, featuring detailed topographic models, historical exhibits about the UNESCO site, and interactive displays on the region's unique granite peaks and pine trees. It's the perfect place to understand the mountain's geology and plan your hiking routes before ascending.
huangshan-front.tip: Study the large 3D terrain model near the entrance to identify which peaks and viewpoints match your fitness level and time constraints before buying cable car tickets.

huangshan-front.name: Huangshan Mountain
huangshan-front.address: Huangshan Mountain, Huangshan District, Huangshan City, China, 245801
huangshan-front.description: Granite peaks rise through shifting seas of clouds, their twisted pine trees clinging to vertical cliffs in gravity-defying arrangements. The otherworldly landscape has inspired centuries of Chinese painters and remains one of nature's most dramatic displays.
huangshan-front.tip: Take the cable car up before dawn and hike down after sunrise to avoid crowds and catch the famous cloud sea at its most photogenic.

huangshan-front.name: Huangshan Moya Stone Engraving
huangshan-front.address: 457G+249 Huangshan Tourism, Tang Kou Zhen, Huang Shan Qu, Huang Shan Shi, An Hui Sheng, China, 245899
huangshan-front.description: Ancient stone inscriptions carved directly into cliff faces along the mountain paths showcase centuries of poetry, calligraphy, and praise from scholars and emperors who climbed Huangshan. These weathered engravings offer a tangible connection to China's literary heritage while framing dramatic mountain vistas.
huangshan-front.tip: Look for the large red-painted characters near Yuping Scenic Area—they're easier to photograph in soft morning light when shadows enhance the carved depth.

huangshan-back.name: Huangshan National Forest Park
huangshan-back.address: China, An Hui Sheng, Huang Shan Shi, Huang Shan Qu, 205国道5835+73W 邮政编码: 245706
huangshan-back.description: This national forest park showcases granite peaks wrapped in ancient pines and mystical clouds that shift throughout the day, creating an ever-changing landscape painters have captured for centuries. The dramatic rock formations and twisted trees perched on impossible cliffs offer photography opportunities unlike anywhere else in China.
huangshan-back.tip: Start your hike before 6am to catch the famous sea of clouds phenomenon and avoid tour groups that arrive mid-morning on the cable cars.

huangshan-back.name: A Monkey Gaping at the Sea
huangshan-back.address: 45VC+R66 Huangshan Tourism, Tang Kou Zhen, Huang Shan Qu, Huang Shan Shi, An Hui Sheng, China, 245801
huangshan-back.description: This famous rock formation perched on a precipice resembles a monkey crouching and gazing out over a rolling sea of clouds. The optical illusion is best appreciated when mist fills the valleys below, creating the appearance of an endless ocean beneath the stone sentinel.
huangshan-back.tip: Visit early morning after rainfall when cloud formations are densest and most dramatic. The viewing platform gets crowded after 9am, so arrive at dawn for clearer photos.

tunxi-oldstreet.name: Tunxi Old Street
tunxi-oldstreet.address: China, Huang Shan Shi, Tun Xi Qu, 东南方向50米 CN 安徽省 黄山市 屯溪区 52-2 邮政编码: 245011
tunxi-oldstreet.description: This 900-year-old pedestrian street stretches over a kilometer with Ming and Qing dynasty architecture housing tea shops, ink stone merchants, and traditional medicine stores. The authentic blend of working businesses and historical preservation offers a rare glimpse into Huizhou commercial culture without the tourist circus found in more famous ancient streets.
tunxi-oldstreet.tip: Visit the Chengkan Hui inkstone workshops in the side alleys where artisans still hand-carve stones using centuries-old techniques, and prices are half what street-front shops charge.

tunxi-oldstreet.name: Huashan Mysterious Grottoes
tunxi-oldstreet.address: China, An Hui Sheng, Huang Shan Shi, Tun Xi Qu, 001县道屯光镇浯村 邮政编码: 245251
tunxi-oldstreet.description: Explore a vast network of ancient hand-carved caverns dating back 1,700 years, featuring towering stone chambers, mysterious pillars, and subterranean pools whose original purpose remains unknown. The sheer scale of these man-made caves, excavated with primitive tools, creates an atmospheric journey through one of China's most puzzling archaeological sites.
tunxi-oldstreet.tip: Visit caves 2 and 35 first as they are the largest and most impressive. The caves stay cool year-round, so bring a light jacket even in summer.

tunxi-oldstreet.name: Huizhou Ancient City
tunxi-oldstreet.address: VC8M+QXV, Hui Zhou Lu, She Xian, Huang Shan Shi, An Hui Sheng, China, 245299
tunxi-oldstreet.summary: Centuries-old district with cobbled streets, temples & archways dating to the Ming & Qing dynasties.
tunxi-oldstreet.description: This Ming and Qing dynasty district preserves traditional Hui-style architecture along cobbled lanes, with ancient temples, memorial archways, and courtyard residences showcasing intricate wood carvings and white-washed walls. The authentic atmosphere of daily life unfolding among centuries-old buildings offers a genuine glimpse into historic Anhui culture.
tunxi-oldstreet.tip: Enter through the east gate early morning to watch locals practicing tai chi in temple courtyards and visit the ancestral halls before tour groups arrive around 9am.

tunxi-oldstreet.name: 花山谜窟景区
tunxi-oldstreet.address: Tunguangzhen, Tunxi District, Huangshan City, Anhui, China, 245051
tunxi-oldstreet.description: These ancient hand-carved grottoes hollowed into granite cliffs remain an archaeological mystery, with 36 cave chambers featuring precise chisel marks and unknown origins dating back 1,700 years. The eerie underground halls, some reaching cathedral-like proportions with stone pillars and reflecting pools, offer a cool escape into one of China's most puzzling historical sites.
tunxi-oldstreet.tip: Visit Cave 35, the largest chamber with water features and dramatic lighting. Wear non-slip shoes as floors can be wet and uneven inside the caves.

tunxi-oldstreet.name: Tangyue
tunxi-oldstreet.address: China, An Hui Sheng, Huang Shan Shi, She Xian, 020乡道V99C+2MG 邮政编码: 245299
tunxi-oldstreet.description: This historic village showcases seven magnificent memorial arches built across four centuries by the Bao clan, forming China's most complete collection of stone paifang architecture. Walk beneath these towering granite structures to understand how Ming and Qing dynasty families commemorated their ancestors' achievements and virtues.
tunxi-oldstreet.tip: Visit the Bao Family Garden behind the arches early morning when tour groups haven't arrived yet. The ancient bonsai collection and traditional Huizhou architecture are best photographed in soft morning light.

tunxi-oldstreet.name: Tunxi Museum
tunxi-oldstreet.address: 168 Lao Jie, Tun Xi Qu, Huang Shan Shi, An Hui Sheng, China, 245011
tunxi-oldstreet.description: This compact museum showcases Huizhou regional artifacts including ancient merchant ledgers, ink stones, and carved wooden panels from Ming and Qing dynasty residences. It offers an excellent primer on local Hui culture before exploring the adjacent old street's architecture and shops.
tunxi-oldstreet.tip: Visit before walking Tunxi Old Street to understand the historical context of the merchant houses and traditional crafts you'll encounter, making the street experience far more meaningful.

tunxi-oldstreet.name: Huangshan Liyang Tourism Zone
tunxi-oldstreet.address: China, An Hui Sheng, Huang Shan Shi, Tun Xi Qu, 003, 正西方向170米 邮政编码: 245021
tunxi-oldstreet.description: This historic waterfront quarter along the Hengjiang River blends Ming and Qing dynasty architecture with contemporary art galleries, craft workshops, and riverside cafes. The illuminated stone bridges and traditional Hui-style buildings create a charming evening atmosphere perfect for leisurely exploration.
tunxi-oldstreet.tip: Visit the small alleyways behind the main street to find family-run tea houses and watch local craftsmen practicing traditional Hui inkstone carving techniques.

hongcun-xidi.name: Hongcun
hongcun-xidi.address: China, An Hui Sheng, Huang Shan Shi, Yi Xian, 030县道2X2R+7HG 邮政编码: 245531
hongcun-xidi.summary: Old-world village, UNESCO site & Ang Lee–movie backdrop with historic art & architecture.
hongcun-xidi.description: This 900-year-old village floats like a watercolor painting with its moon pond reflecting whitewashed Hui-style homes where Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon filmed its most memorable scenes. Walking through the maze of narrow alleyways reveals ancient wooden carvings and a sophisticated Ming Dynasty water system still flowing beneath your feet.
hongcun-xidi.tip: Arrive before 8am to photograph the South Lake before tour groups flood in, and skip the pricey village cafes for authentic local snacks from street vendors near the north entrance.

hongcun-xidi.name: Hongcun Scenic Area
hongcun-xidi.address: West Gate of Hongcun Scenic Area, 2X3M+FHM黟县 Yi County, 安徽省 China, 245531
hongcun-xidi.description: This 900-year-old village resembles a traditional Chinese ink painting, with moon-shaped ponds reflecting white-walled Huizhou architecture and narrow stone lanes winding between ancestral halls. The UNESCO-listed village served as a filming location for Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon, bringing its timeless water system and merchant mansions to life.
hongcun-xidi.tip: Arrive by 7am before tour groups flood in, or stay overnight at a guesthouse to experience the village's serene early morning mist over South Lake.

hongcun-xidi.name: Xidi Ancient Dwellings Travel
hongcun-xidi.address: China, An Hui Sheng, Huang Shan Shi, Yi Xian, 032乡道西递镇 邮政编码: 245501
hongcun-xidi.description: This UNESCO World Heritage village showcases over 120 Huizhou-style Ming and Qing dynasty residences with intricate stone carvings, horsehead walls, and tranquil courtyards. The remarkably preserved architecture offers an authentic glimpse into centuries-old Chinese merchant life and traditional feng shui principles.
hongcun-xidi.tip: Visit the履福堂 (Lüfu Hall) early morning when soft light streams through wooden lattice windows, creating perfect conditions for photography before tour groups arrive around 9am.

hongcun-xidi.name: Mukeng Zhuhai
hongcun-xidi.address: China, An Hui Sheng, Huang Shan Shi, Yi Xian, 102乡道宏村木坑 邮政编码: 245531
hongcun-xidi.description: A sea of towering bamboo forests carpets the hillsides near Hongcun, creating corridors of emerald green that sway and whisper in the breeze. The otherworldly landscape gained fame as a filming location for Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon, offering photographers dreamlike compositions at every turn.
hongcun-xidi.tip: Visit early morning when mist weaves through the bamboo groves, creating an ethereal atmosphere perfect for photography. Follow the elevated boardwalk trail for the best elevated views over the bamboo canopy.

hongcun-xidi.name: Xidi
hongcun-xidi.address: Xidi, WX3R+W27, Xidi, 黟县黄山市 China, 245501
hongcun-xidi.description: This UNESCO-listed village showcases nearly 300 Ming and Qing dynasty residences with intricate stone carvings, wooden lattice windows, and ornate horsehead walls that once housed prosperous merchant families. The remarkably preserved ancestral halls and narrow alleyways offer an authentic glimpse into Huizhou architectural traditions spanning six centuries.
hongcun-xidi.tip: Enter through the less-crowded eastern gate near Zoushi Huayuan to avoid tour groups. Visit residential courtyards in the afternoon when most tour buses have departed for better photo opportunities.

zhongshan-scenic.name: Dr. Sun Yat-sen's Mausoleum
zhongshan-scenic.address: China, Jiang Su Sheng, Nan Jing Shi, Xuan Wu Qu, 石祥路7号 邮政编码: 210014
zhongshan-scenic.summary: A steep staircase leads to this ornately designed mausoleum for one of the founders of modern China.
zhongshan-scenic.description: This grand mausoleum honors Sun Yat-sen, revered across Asia as the father of modern China, with 392 marble steps ascending through forested Purple Mountain to a magnificent blue-tiled memorial hall. The architectural blend of Chinese and Western styles reflects Sun's revolutionary vision that shaped the region's entire 20th century.
zhongshan-scenic.tip: Arrive right at 8:30am opening to climb the stairs before crowds and heat. The steps appear endless from below but landings strategically break the ascent—count them for distraction.

zhongshan-scenic.name: City Wall of Nanjing
zhongshan-scenic.address: 2RGG+942, Lan Qi Jie, Qin Huai Qu, Nan Jing Shi, Jiang Su Sheng, China, 210095
zhongshan-scenic.summary: World's longest brick city wall, built in the Ming dynasty, stretching 15.5 mi. with 13 gates.
zhongshan-scenic.description: This Ming dynasty marvel stands as the world's longest brick city wall, stretching 15.5 miles with massive gates and ramparts that once protected an imperial capital. Walking or cycling atop its broad stone pathway offers an unmatched journey through 600 years of military architecture and urban history.
zhongshan-scenic.tip: Rent a bicycle at Zhonghua Gate to cover more ground along the wall's walkable sections, especially the scenic stretch between Zhonghua and Xuanwu Lake where crowds thin out considerably.

zhongshan-scenic.name: Zhongshan Mountain National Park
zhongshan-scenic.address: Xuanwu, Nanjing, China
zhongshan-scenic.description: This expansive mountain park encompasses forested trails, historic sites including Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum, and panoramic views over Nanjing from its peaks. The combination of natural beauty and cultural significance across multiple connected attractions makes it the city's premier outdoor destination.
zhongshan-scenic.tip: Enter from the east gate near Linggu Temple early morning to avoid crowds at the main mausoleum entrance, and explore the quieter forest paths with fewer tour groups.

zhongshan-scenic.name: Zhongshanmen
zhongshan-scenic.address: 2RQG+8RW, Ning Hang Gong Lu, Bai Xia Qu, Nan Jing Shi, Jiang Su Sheng, China, 210095
zhongshan-scenic.description: This historic Ming Dynasty city gate stands as a grand entrance to Nanjing's ancient walls, featuring massive stone archways and original defensive architecture. The fortification offers an authentic glimpse into how imperial China protected its eastern capital, with well-preserved ramparts you can walk along.
zhongshan-scenic.tip: Climb the adjacent city wall section for panoramic views of both old and new Nanjing. Visit early morning when local residents practice tai chi near the gate's base.

zhongshan-scenic.name: Toutuoling Scenic Area
zhongshan-scenic.address: 3R9R+WXV, Xuanwu, Nanjing, Jiangsu, China, 210042
zhongshan-scenic.description: This forested ridgeline in the Zhongshan Scenic Area offers panoramic views of Nanjing's eastern hills and Zijin Mountain through a network of stone pathways and viewing platforms. The peaceful trails and fresh mountain air make it ideal for escaping the city without leaving its boundaries.
zhongshan-scenic.tip: Start your hike early morning from the eastern entrance near Linggu Temple to avoid crowds and catch sunrise views over the valley.

zhongshan-scenic.name: Nanjing Botanical Garden, Memorial Sun Yat-Sen
zhongshan-scenic.address: Xuanwu, Nanjing, China, 210016
zhongshan-scenic.summary: Renowned national botanical garden, with 5 research centers & 1000s of species on display.
zhongshan-scenic.description: This premier botanical garden houses over 3,000 plant species across specialized zones, from medicinal herb collections to rare tropical palms, all set within the historic Zhongshan scenic area. The specialized research gardens showcase plants rarely seen elsewhere in China, including endangered species conservation areas that double as peaceful walking trails.
zhongshan-scenic.tip: Visit the Red Leaf Valley section in late November for spectacular autumn foliage without the crowds that flock to nearby Xiaoling tomb areas.

zhongshan-scenic.name: Zhongshan Scenic Area
zhongshan-scenic.address: China, 江苏省南京市栖霞区3VG3+C3Q 邮政编码: 210042
zhongshan-scenic.description: This expansive mountain park encompasses Sun Yat-sen's Mausoleum, Ming Xiaoling tomb, and forested trails covering Purple Mountain's slopes. The blend of republican-era architecture, ancient imperial tombs, and natural landscapes offers a comprehensive glimpse into Nanjing's layered history.
zhongshan-scenic.tip: Start at the eastern Linggu Temple entrance early morning to avoid tour groups that flood Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum from 10am onwards, then work your way west.

zhongshan-scenic.name: Dr. Sun Yat-sen's Mausoleum Scenic Area （West Gate）
zhongshan-scenic.address: 3V43+C44, Ling Yuan Lu, Xuan Wu Qu, Nan Jing Shi, Jiang Su Sheng, China, 210014
zhongshan-scenic.description: This grand mausoleum honors the founding father of modern China, featuring 392 stone steps leading up to a majestic blue-tiled memorial hall set against the forested slopes of Purple Mountain. The architectural blend of traditional Chinese and Western design elements creates a powerful tribute that draws millions of visitors paying their respects.
zhongshan-scenic.tip: Arrive before 9am to avoid tour group crowds and climb the steps in cooler temperatures. The west gate offers easier access to the main mausoleum area than other entrances.

zhongshan-scenic.name: Ming Xiaoling Mausoleum
zhongshan-scenic.address: 3R5M+7RP, Xuanwu, Nanjing, Jiangsu, China, 210000
zhongshan-scenic.summary: Massive mausoleum built in 1405 for Ming Emperor Zhu Yuanzhang & featuring many pathways & statues.
zhongshan-scenic.description: This UNESCO World Heritage mausoleum of the Ming Dynasty's founding emperor features a sacred 600-meter Spirit Way lined with imposing stone warriors, mythical beasts, and elephants carved from single blocks. The tomb complex showcases Ming architecture at its grandest, with ancient cypress trees framing structures that influenced all imperial tombs that followed.
zhongshan-scenic.tip: Visit the Spirit Way first thing when gates open to photograph the stone guardians in soft morning light without crowds, then explore the less-visited western burial mound area.

zhongshan-scenic.name: Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum Music Stage
zhongshan-scenic.address: China, Jiang Su Sheng, Nan Jing Shi, Xuan Wu Qu, 中山陵风景区 邮政编码: 210042
zhongshan-scenic.description: This semi-circular stone stage sits amid forested hillsides, designed in 1932 as an open-air amphitheater with exceptional natural acoustics. Visit to experience the graceful symmetry of its classical Chinese architecture and test the famous acoustic phenomenon from the center stage.
zhongshan-scenic.tip: Stand at the center marker on stage and speak normally—the curved walls amplify your voice remarkably while observers just meters away hear nothing, creating a surreal acoustic effect.

zhongshan-scenic.name: Zhongshan National Park
zhongshan-scenic.address: China, Jiang Su Sheng, 7, Xuan Wu Qu, 石象路7号 邮政编码: 210096
zhongshan-scenic.description: This expansive forested park encompasses purple mountain slopes, ancient temple ruins, and shaded hiking trails connecting Nanjing's most significant historical sites. The serene woodland setting offers a peaceful escape from urban Nanjing while providing access to multiple cultural landmarks within one continuous green space.
zhongshan-scenic.tip: Enter from the eastern gate near Ming Xiaoling Mausoleum early morning to hike the quieter forest trails before crowds arrive at the main monuments.

zhongshan-scenic.name: Nanjing Xianlinhu Park
zhongshan-scenic.address: 4XGP+VC4, Wei Di Lu, Qi Xia Qu, Nan Jing Shi, Jiang Su Sheng, China, 210033
zhongshan-scenic.description: This lakeside park in Nanjing's university district features a picturesque 3.8-kilometer walking path encircling a pristine freshwater lake, surrounded by metarows and willow trees. The peaceful waterfront promenade offers a refreshing escape from urban crowds, with plenty of spots for cycling, jogging, or simply watching local fishermen at work.
zhongshan-scenic.tip: Visit early morning to see local residents practicing tai chi and traditional dance along the eastern shore, where you can join in or simply observe authentic community life.

zhongshan-scenic.name: Qixia Mountain Scenic Spot
zhongshan-scenic.address: 88 Qi Xia Jie, Qi Xia Qu, Nan Jing Shi, Jiang Su Sheng, China, 210033
zhongshan-scenic.description: Ancient Buddhist grottoes carved into crimson cliffs meet pavilions scattered across forested slopes, where maple trees ignite the mountainside each autumn in brilliant reds and golds. The Thousand Buddha Caves, dating back 1,500 years, showcase some of southern China's finest stone Buddhist sculptures.
zhongshan-scenic.tip: Visit the Ming Mirror Lake area early morning when mist rises off the water, creating ethereal reflections of the mountain. Avoid weekends during autumn foliage season when crowds surge.

zhongshan-scenic.name: Nanjing Ten Dynasties History Culture Park
zhongshan-scenic.address: China, Jiang Su Sheng, Nan Jing Shi, Xuan Wu Qu, Ling Yuan Lu, 中山陵园景区内 邮政编码: 210014
zhongshan-scenic.description: This cultural park traces Nanjing's role as capital through ten different dynasties with pavilions, stone monuments, and archaeological displays set along landscaped grounds. It offers a concentrated journey through centuries of imperial history without the crowds of major palace sites.
zhongshan-scenic.tip: Visit the western section first where Ming and Qing dynasty relics are concentrated, then work chronologically backwards through the park's layout to better understand the historical progression.

zhongshan-scenic.name: Nanjing Qiqiaoweng Wetland Park
zhongshan-scenic.address: 2R4R+659, You Yi He Lu, Qin Huai Qu, Nan Jing Shi, Jiang Su Sheng, China, 210007
zhongshan-scenic.description: This ecological wetland park features restored marshlands with wooden boardwalks winding through reed beds, lotus ponds, and seasonal migratory bird habitats. Visit to experience a peaceful nature retreat within Nanjing's city limits, where you can observe native waterfowl and enjoy serene lakeside views.
zhongshan-scenic.tip: Visit during early morning hours in spring or autumn migration seasons to spot the greatest variety of waterbirds, including herons and egrets feeding in the shallow marshes.

zhongshan-scenic.name: 南京人化石地点
zhongshan-scenic.address: China, Jiang Su Sheng, Nan Jing Shi, Jiang Ning Qu, 汤山镇雷公山下 邮政编码: 211131
zhongshan-scenic.description: This archaeological site preserves evidence of Nanjing Man, ancient human fossils dating back approximately 600,000 years discovered in limestone caves. Visit to witness where some of China's earliest human ancestors once lived and understand the deep prehistoric roots of the Yangtze River region.
zhongshan-scenic.tip: The site is relatively undeveloped with minimal signage. Consider hiring a local guide or reading about the discovery beforehand to fully appreciate the paleontological significance of this location.

zhongshan-scenic.name: Nanjing Ape Cave Scenic Area
zhongshan-scenic.address: China, Jiang Su Sheng, Nan Jing Shi, Jiang Ning Qu, 汤泉西路汤山镇 邮政编码: 211131
zhongshan-scenic.description: This prehistoric cave site preserves evidence of early human habitation dating back hundreds of thousands of years, with fossil displays and reconstructed living scenes bringing ancient history to life. The cave's cool interior and surrounding forested trails offer a peaceful escape while exploring humanity's origins in the Yangtze River region.
zhongshan-scenic.tip: Visit on weekday mornings to avoid school groups. The cave temperature stays around 15°C year-round, so bring a light jacket even in summer.

zhongshan-scenic.name: Xiamafang Relics Park
zhongshan-scenic.address: 2RQW+GQF, Zhong Shan Men Da Jie, Xuan Wu Qu, Nan Jing Shi, Jiang Su Sheng, China, 210014
zhongshan-scenic.description: This archaeological park preserves the monumental stone archway and sacred way remnants from the Ming Dynasty's imperial tomb approach, where officials once dismounted from horses as a sign of respect. The massive carved stone pillars and mythical animal statues offer an intimate glimpse into imperial funeral traditions without the crowds of larger tomb sites.
zhongshan-scenic.tip: Visit early morning when local tai chi practitioners gather around the ancient stones, creating an atmospheric scene perfect for photography with soft golden light illuminating the weathered carvings.

zhongshan-scenic.name: Nanjing Shixiang Road
zhongshan-scenic.address: 2RWR+XV5, Shi Xiang Lu, Xuan Wu Qu, Nan Jing Shi, Jiang Su Sheng, China, 210014
zhongshan-scenic.description: This ancient sacred pathway is lined with monumental Ming Dynasty stone sculptures of elephants, lions, camels, and mythical beasts that once guarded the approach to imperial tombs. The atmospheric tree-canopied avenue creates a mystical journey through 600 years of history, especially enchanting during autumn when golden ginkgo leaves blanket the stones.
zhongshan-scenic.tip: Visit early morning on weekdays to photograph the stone animals with dramatic side-lighting and minimal crowds. The autumn ginkgo season draws huge weekend crowds, so timing is essential.

zhongshan-scenic.name: Beiting
zhongshan-scenic.address: 3V63+594, Xuanwu, Nanjing, Jiangsu, China, 210042
zhongshan-scenic.description: This historic stone pavilion stands as a monument marker within the Zhongshan Scenic Area, featuring traditional architectural elements and inscribed stone tablets that commemorate significant historical events. The pavilion offers a quiet moment of reflection amid the larger memorial complex, connecting visitors to centuries of Chinese commemorative tradition.
zhongshan-scenic.tip: Visit early morning when tour groups haven't arrived yet for peaceful photography and to observe local elderly residents practicing traditional exercises nearby.

zhongshan-scenic.name: Wenwu Fangmen
zhongshan-scenic.address: 3R3Q+WW4, Xuanwu, Nanjing, Jiangsu, China, 210014
zhongshan-scenic.description: This historic stone gateway stands as one of the original ceremonial entrances to the Ming Dynasty imperial complex in Zhongshan Scenic Area. The ancient archway's weathered stone carvings and commanding presence offer a tangible connection to Nanjing's imperial past.
zhongshan-scenic.tip: Visit early morning when tourist groups haven't arrived yet—the eastern light creates dramatic shadows through the archway, perfect for photography without crowds blocking your shots.

city-north.name: Niushoushan Cultural Tourism Zone
city-north.address: China, Jiang Su Sheng, Nan Jing Shi, Jiang Ning Qu, 宁丹大道18号 邮政编码: 211199
city-north.summary: Huge religious site with a 9-tiered pagoda & striking domed palace housing a sacred Buddhist relic.
city-north.description: This vast Buddhist complex centers around the golden-domed Usnisa Palace, built to enshrine a sacred skull bone relic of Shakyamuni Buddha beneath its spectacular underground chambers. The nine-story Usnisa Pagoda and meticulously landscaped grounds create one of China's most ambitious modern religious architecture projects.
city-north.tip: Visit the underground palace in the afternoon when tour groups thin out—the intricate mandala ceilings and relic chamber are best appreciated without crowds rushing you through.

city-north.name: Yuantoushi Wet Land Scenic Area
city-north.address: XMQC+5F7, Jianye District, Nanjing, Jiangsu, China, 211808
city-north.description: This expansive wetland park at the confluence of the Qinhuai and Yangtze Rivers features winding boardwalks through reed beds and observation platforms for migratory bird watching. The panoramic river views and tranquil wetland ecosystem offer a peaceful escape from urban Nanjing.
city-north.tip: Visit during early morning hours in spring or autumn migration seasons to spot rare waterbirds like egrets and herons feeding in the shallow waters.

city-north.name: Foding Tower
city-north.address: WP6R+GCV, Jiangning District, Nanjing, Jiangsu, China, 210041
city-north.description: This striking pagoda rises above Nanjing's northern landscape as part of the Niushou Mountain Buddhist complex, featuring intricate architectural details and golden spires that reflect traditional Tang dynasty design. The tower's observation levels offer sweeping views across the city and surrounding hills, making it a rewarding climb for both spiritual seekers and photographers.
city-north.tip: Visit during late afternoon when the golden exterior catches the sunset light, creating exceptional photo opportunities without the midday crowds that arrive on tour buses.

city-north.name: Youth Olympic Forest Park
city-north.address: XMWX+WJF, Jiang Di Lu, Jian Ye Qu, Nan Jing Shi, Jiang Su Sheng, China, 220019
city-north.description: This expansive green space along the Yangtze River was built for the 2014 Youth Olympics, featuring modern sculptures, cycling paths, and riverside meadows perfect for picnics. The elevated observation deck offers sweeping views across the river to Nanjing's northern districts, making it ideal for sunset watching.
city-north.tip: Rent a bicycle from the east entrance to cover more ground—the park stretches over 4 kilometers and walking the entire loop can take hours.

city-north.name: Niushou Mountain Forest Farm
city-north.address: WQ63+JJV, Jiangning District, Nanjing, Jiangsu, China, 211199
city-north.description: This working forest farm on the slopes of Niushou Mountain offers peaceful hiking trails through bamboo groves and pine forests, far from the tourist crowds at the main scenic area. The farm's quiet paths and fresh mountain air make it perfect for locals seeking an authentic nature escape without entrance fees.
city-north.tip: Enter from the eastern side near Fodingjin village to access the best forest trails. Local farmers sometimes sell fresh seasonal produce at the trailheads during weekends.

city-north.name: Nanjing Foshou Lake Country Park
city-north.description: This lakeside park in northern Nanjing features walking trails around the Buddha's Hand-shaped lake, with wetland ecosystems and willow-lined shores. The peaceful atmosphere and natural scenery provide an authentic escape from urban crowds without leaving the city.
city-north.tip: Visit early morning to see local residents practicing tai chi by the water and catch the best light for photographing the lake's distinctive shape from elevated viewpoints.

city-north.name: Nanjing Chengqiang Qingliangmen
city-north.address: 2QX4+3VC, Qing Liang Men Da Jie, Gu Lou Qu, Nan Jing Shi, Jiang Su Sheng, China, 210029
city-north.description: Qingliangmen is a well-preserved Ming Dynasty gate that forms part of Nanjing's ancient city wall, featuring traditional defensive architecture with massive stone blocks and a towering archway. The gate offers an authentic glimpse into medieval Chinese military engineering without the crowds found at more famous wall sections.
city-north.tip: Visit the small museum inside the gatehouse to see historical photos and artifacts, then climb to the ramparts for views of the northern residential neighborhoods rarely seen by tourists.

city-north.name: 南京眼
city-north.address: XPV3+X8M, Nan Jing Yan Bu Xing Qiao, Jian Ye Qu, Nan Jing Shi, Jiang Su Sheng, China, 220019
city-north.description: This elegant pedestrian bridge spans the Yangtze River with an asymmetrical eye-shaped design featuring illuminated steel cables that create dramatic patterns at night. The elevated walkway offers expansive river views and connects Nanjing's riverside parks, making it a favorite spot for evening strolls and photography.
city-north.tip: Visit after sunset when the LED lights transform the bridge into a glowing spectacle. Walk to the middle section for the best symmetrical photos of the cable patterns.

city-north.name: Nanjing Laoshan National Park
city-north.chinese: 南京老山国家森林公园
city-north.description: This forested mountain park in northern Nanjing offers hiking trails through bamboo groves and pine forests, with pavilions overlooking the Yangtze River valley. The fresh air and peaceful woodland environment provide a natural escape from urban Nanjing without traveling far from the city center.
city-north.tip: Visit the western trails early morning to spot local birdwatchers and tai chi practitioners gathering near the mountain springs before crowds arrive on weekends.

city-north.name: 南京莫愁湖公园
city-north.description: This classical garden park centers around a serene lake named after a legendary Tang Dynasty woman, featuring ancient pavilions, zigzagging bridges, and centuries-old lotus ponds. The best reason to visit is witnessing the spectacular summer lotus blooms that transform the lake into a sea of pink and white flowers.
city-north.tip: Visit the Shengqi Building in the northwest corner for the best panoramic views of the lotus-covered lake, especially during early morning when the flowers are freshest and crowds are minimal.

qinhuai-district.name: Nanjing Confucius Temple-The Qinhuai River Scenic Area
qinhuai-district.chinese: 南京夫子庙-秦淮河风光带
qinhuai-district.address: China, Jiang Su Sheng, Nan Jing Shi, Qin Huai Qu, 夫子庙夫子庙景区步行街2QCR+MPQ 邮政编码: 210001
qinhuai-district.description: This historic riverside complex blends ancient temple architecture with bustling markets along the illuminated Qinhuai River, where painted pleasure boats once carried scholars and poets. The evening lantern displays transform the waterway into a glowing spectacle that has captivated visitors for over 1,500 years.
qinhuai-district.tip: Visit after 6pm when the red lanterns illuminate the buildings and river. Board a painted boat near Wende Bridge for the best photo angles of the temple complex reflected in the water.

qinhuai-district.name: Laomendong
qinhuai-district.address: China, Nan Jing Shi, 与 Jian Zi Xiang, 箍桶巷交汇处 邮政编码: 210004
qinhuai-district.description: This reconstructed historic quarter showcases Ming and Qing dynasty architecture along narrow stone lanes filled with traditional teahouses, craft shops, and snack vendors. The blend of authentic Nanjing street food culture with beautifully preserved courtyard buildings offers an immersive glimpse into the city's old commercial district.
qinhuai-district.tip: Visit the small Jiang Xianghe Former Residence tucked in the eastern section for a quieter cultural experience away from the main pedestrian flow.

qinhuai-district.name: 瞻园
qinhuai-district.address: 128 Zhan Yuan Lu, 夫子庙地区 Qin Huai Qu, Nan Jing Shi, Jiang Su Sheng, China, 210004
qinhuai-district.summary: Verdant urban Chinese garden was built during the early Ming dynasty & known for its flora & fauna.
qinhuai-district.description: This Ming dynasty garden showcases classical southern Chinese landscaping with rockeries, lotus ponds, and winding corridors that once belonged to the first Ming emperor's general. The intricate stone arrangements and traditional pavilions offer an authentic glimpse into imperial-era garden design without the crowds of larger tourist sites.
qinhuai-district.tip: Visit during early morning hours when local retirees practice tai chi by the main pond, creating an atmospheric scene that captures traditional Nanjing life.

qinhuai-district.name: Gate of China, Nanjing
qinhuai-district.address: 2Q7J+3P2, Zhong Hua Lu, Qin Huai Qu, Nan Jing Shi, Jiang Su Sheng, China, 210004
